question: Choose the option that best completes the gap, I'll find time for my _________ when I get _________with this difficult assignment.
solution: Pastime implies an activity that someone does regularly for enjoyment rather than work , while get through implies to pass a difficult experience
subject: English Language

question: After so many trials, the experiment __________
solution: Paid off means to become worthwhile or to produce a benefit.
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option that best completes the gap. The chairman refused to shake __________ with the secretary
solution: When you shake hands with someone you take their right hand in your own for a few moments, often moving it up and down slightly
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option that best completes the gap. Wherever the leader went people struggled to catch a __________ of him
solution: To catch a glimpse of someone or something means to briefly look at someone or to briefly see the person or thing. The verb ‘catch’ cannot be used with ‘look’ in option B, ‘view’ in option C and ‘picture’ in option D.
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option that best completes the gap. Mrs. Okoro __________ in this school since 1975
solution: 'Since' implies from a specified time in the past. A present perfect progressive tense describes an event in a progressive form that started in the past and ended in the present. 'has been teaching' is an example of a verb element associated with a present perfect progressive tense
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option that best completes the gap.  I was seriously disappointed when the __________ between the two teams ended in a goalless draw
solution: A match means a competitive sport event between two teams
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option that best completes the gap. The young man looked carefully at the long document, but he couldn't make __________ what it meant
solution: To make out;  means to understand something. Simply put, he couldn't understand what it meant.
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option that best completes the gap. While the worshippers closed their eyes in prayer, a thief made __________ with the collection
solution: Made away means to steal or abduct
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the gap.

If you keep playing with this door handle, it will get __________
solution: The sentence refers to the door handle becoming "loose", which means not tightly fixed or secure. This is the correct word to describe the state of the handle. "Loose" here is used as an adjective to describe the state of the door handle if it continues to be played with.
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option nearest in meaning to the underlined. For all he cared: the game was as good as lost
solution:
'For all I care' is used to say that a person is not worried/ uninterested about what happened to someone or something. 'As good as' indicates a comparison between 'the game' and 'a lost'

Option C is the best answer

options.a: He did not care if the game as lost
options.b: He could not care less if the game was lost
options.c: He was almost certain that the game would be lost
options.d: He was afraid the game would be lost

subject: English Language

question: Choose the option nearest in meaning to the underlined. The meeting was cancelled because of the convocation
solution:
To cancel implies to decide/ announce that an event will not take place.

Call off means to decide that something will not happen
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option nearest in meaning to the underlined. Ayodeji is an ardent supporter of education for the child
solution: Ardent means very passionate and enthusiastic
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option nearest in meaning to the underlined. The exhibition was an eye opener to all
solution: Exhibition means a public display of woks of art or items of interest
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option nearest in meaning to the underlined. As a Journalist, Bola has always had a nose for stories
solution: To have a nose for something means to have an instinct or a natural ability to find/ recognize something
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option nearest in meaning to the underlined. Nwankwo was on the verge of signing a two-year contract with the club
solution:
To be on the verge of something means to be very close to it.

To be on the brink also means to be on the edge or border of something.
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option nearest in meaning to the underlined. The Governor told the chief that he was only on a routine tour
solution: Routine means performed as part of a regular procedure
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option nearest in meaning to the underlined. The new ruler is big-hearted in his dealing with the people
solution: Big-hearted means kind and generous
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option nearest in meaning to the underlined. The essay topic is nebulous
solution: Nebulous means vague or unclear
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. The events of last Friday show that there is no love lost between the Principal and the Vice-principal
solution: The term no love lost signifies ill will exclusively. If there is no love lost between two people, they have a strong enmity towards or hate for the other and make no effort to conceal it.
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. If he were here, it could be more fun
solution:
The first clause 'if he were here' reveals an unreal conditional statement that signifies the absence of someone. However the second clause 'it could be more fun'  signifies the presence of little fun.

Option A is the best answer

options.a: He did not show up and so the occasion lacked much fun
options.b: He was being expected to supply more fun
options.c: There was no fun because he was not present
options.d: He was expected but did not show up to liven up the occasion

subject: English Language

question: Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. Adawo is an Imp
solution:
An imp is a person with mischievous behaviour.

Option D is the correct answer.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

The man reasoned that there ought to be a limit to sycophancy
solution:
sycophancy;  using of flattery compliments to gain self-serving favour or advantage from another person.

Option C is correct. 
''The man reasoned that there ought to be a limit to sycophancy'' means that, they should be a limit to how people use flattery compliments on people especially when they stand to gain something in return.

options.a: there can be no favour beyond a reasonable point
options.b: sycophants need not talk all the time
options.c: people should know when not to use flattery
options.d: sycophants should know when to grant people requests

subject: English Language

question: Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. The organization is constantly in a state of flux
solution: A state of flux means a state of uncertainty about what should be done usually followed by the establishment of a new direction of action, Flux is used to describe something that constantly changes

options.a: There are periodic changes in the organization
options.b: The organization is facing a difficult period
options.c: The organization is experiencing; good times

subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) or phrase in italics.

The witness averred that she had seen Dosun at the scene of the crime
solution:
To aver means to establish a fact or assert a truth.

To deny means to disprove of a statement. It is the opposite of ‘aver’.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) or phrase in italics.

The governor's visit is an unprecedented event in the history of the social club
solution:
unprecedented; means something that has never done or known before.
Something that is remarkable.

Option C ''Insignificant'' is the correct option. It means simply means something that is meaningless; too small or unimportant to be worth consideration.
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) or phrase in italics. Our principal took exception to the ignoble role the teacher played in the matter
solution: ignoble implies not honourable in character or purpose
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) or phrase in italics. He is notorious for his drunkenness
solution:
Notorious means famous or well known for some bad qualities.

Renowned means famous for something for good qualities
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) or phrase in italics.

The severity of the harmattan helped me to complete my writing assignment in record time because i had no choice but to lock myself indoors
solution: severity is the state of being severe . the degree of something being bad or undesirable very great; intense. while the opposite of severity is mildness which means not severe; gentle.
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) or phrase in italics. This card entitles you to attend the film show
solution:
‘Entitle’ means to qualify, allow or give someone the right to do something

‘Disqualify’ means to make one ineligible to do something.
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) or phrase in italics. He is loved for his altruism
solution:
Altruism means to have regard for others at the expense of oneself.

Selfishness means to always consider one’s own interest before others.
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) or phrase in italics. The Journalist said he was working freelance
solution:
To work freelance means to work randomly without having a long-term contract with any firm or person.

Dependent means to rely on a particular person or thing. It is the antonym of ‘freelance’ in this context.
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) or phrase in italics. The Flying Eagles put up a plucky defence against their opponents
solution:
Plucky means showing courage in difficult situations.

Weak means lacking energy or force.
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) or phrase in italics. We found a shady place for the display
solution: Shady means situated in or full of shade
subject: English Language

question: Choose the word(s) or phrase(s) which best fill(s) the gap. The sea wave continue to ____________ the cliff on the west coast constantly
solution: Erode means to gradually wear away or gradually disappear. Erode is used in a context that relates to wind, water or other natural agents.
subject: English Language

question: Choose the word(s) or phrase(s) which best fill(s) the gap. I would have been surprised if you ____________ the orange
solution: 'Would have' is used in conditional statements to talk about something that did not happen in the past. In the sequence of tenses, if the main clause contains 'would have', the conditional subordinate clause should be in its past participle.
subject: English Language

question: Choose the word(s) or phrase(s) which best fill(s) the gap. By the time the plane reaches Harare, it __________in the air for twelve hours
solution: 'By the time' is an adverb of time that can be used in the present, past and future tense. The question is, however, in the present tense which will be needing a present perfect tense to complete the sentence.
subject: English Language

question: Choose the word(s) or phrase(s) which best fill(s) the gap. The patient is suffering from ____________
solution: The options contain miss spellings of the word, RHEUMATISM.
subject: English Language

question: Choose the word(s) or phrase(s) which best fill(s) the gap. We saw Ifueko ____________ an egg on the wall
solution:
‘Smash’ is the correct answer because the action conveyed is a single action.

In reported statements, writers/ speakers should retain the present tense.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the word(s) or phrase(s) which best fill(s) the gap.

Knowledge of figures of speech as well as of idiom and lexical items ____________ in this section
solution:
When either of these words is used; as well as, together with, alongside, no less than e.t.c, the subject of the clause would be the noun or pronoun that comes before the marker of accompaniment. 

Knowledge of figures of speech is a singular subject that should agree with a singular verb
subject: English Language

question: Choose the word(s) or phrase(s) which best fill(s) the gap. The answers to the questions were discussed ____________ the students
solution: 'By' is the best answer to this question. Between can not be considered because the question did not explicitly tell us the number of students involved.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the word(s) or phrase(s) which best fill(s) the gap.

The shipping company advertised for ____________ accountants
solution: The shipping company advertised for two young, but highly competent Nigerian accountants

options.a: two young but highly competent Nigerian
options.b: young but highly competent two Nigerian
options.c: two Nigerian, young, but highly competent
options.d: highly competent, but young, two Nigerian

subject: English Language

question: Choose the word(s) or phrase(s) which best fill(s) the gap. If it had rained, we would have had to postpone the concert. It didn't rain, so ____________
solution: The conditional statement ended with the first sentence. The following statement should therefore end with a past tense
subject: English Language

question: Choose the word(s) or phrase(s) which best fill(s) the gap. Now, I am used ____________ bread without butter
solution:
eating is present participle. 

used is an adjective that qualiffied the verb eating. therefore it is is right to say ''to eating'' rightly fits into that statement

I am used to eating bread without butter.
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option that has a different stress pattern from the others.
solution:
1. ability has its stress pattern on the second syllable

2. registration has its stress pattern on the third syllable

3. quadruplicate has its stress pattern on the second syllable

4. revivalist has its stress pattern on the second syllable

 

Registration, therefore, has a different stress pattern from the rest with its stress on the 2nd syllable
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option that has a different stress pattern from the others.
solution: All the words are stressed on the first syllable except 'represent' which is stressed on the last syllable.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that has the same consonant sound as the one represented by the letter(s) underlined.

thatch
solution: 'Method' has the same consonant sound as the letters underlined in the word 'thatch'
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that has the same consonant sound as the one represented by the letter(s) underlined.

chateau
solution:
chateau; /ˈʃatəʊ/

champagne; /ʃamˈpeɪn/
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that has the same consonant sound as the one represented by the letter(s) emboldened.

Joint
solution:
Joint /dʒɔɪnt/

Advantage /əd.ˈvɑːn.tɪdʒ/
subject: English Language

question: choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) in italics. The manager commended the players for the narrow victory.
solution: Narrow here means close or small margin.
subject: English Language

question:
choose the option that best completes the gap(s).

  I am not sure that all _____ well with John.
solution:
All is well is when it refers to everything.

The question, all is well is most appropriate because the context is used to refer to everything and not everyone in a group
subject: English Language

question: choose the option nearest in meaning to the underlined. Thousands of students are candidates for the examination but only very few pass
solution: Enter for implies to partake, participate or take part in something
subject: English Language

question: choose the option that best completes the gap(s). We look forward to _______ from you very soon.
solution: The expression ‘look forward to’ must be followed with a gerund.
subject: English Language

question:
choose the option nearest in meaning to the italicized words or expressions.

  The French classes were always funny because we considered French a strange tongue.
solution:
Strange means unfamiliar.

Foreign means originating from another country.
subject: English Language

question:
choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) in italics.

  Everyone testifies to the unique taste of our flavor.
subject: English Language

question: Choose the option that best completes the gap. All God's prophets were given the great __________ to preach salvation to people
solution:
Commission means a task or mission to accomplish.

Take note of the double ‘s’ and the double ‘m’. All other options are wrongly spelt
subject: English Language

question:
choose the option that best completes the gap(s).

The principal _________ our mobile phones.
solution:
Note the correct spelling of ‘seize’.

To seize a thing means to deliberately take hold of it for a period of time
subject: English Language

question:
choose the word(s) or phrase(s) which best fill(s) the gap(s).

For fear of being caught, the thief shouted and cursed the slow bank manager in a language ________much for words.
solution:
"For fear of being caught, the thief shouted and cursed the slow bank manager in a language too much for words." 

The phrase “too much for words” is an idiomatic expression used to describe something so intense or extreme that it's difficult or impossible to describe. In this context, the thief’s language (probably abusive or vulgar) was too extreme to be expressed properly in words.
subject: English Language

question:
choose the option nearest in meaning to the italicized words or expressions.

  The coach invited me to his office having found me mettlesome
solution: Mettlesome means to be courageous. The correct answer is A
subject: English Language

question:
choose the option that best completes the gap(s).

You may now ___________ with your presentation.
solution: Note the spelling of each word.
subject: English Language

question:
choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) in italics.

  Father Francis thanked God for the abundance of blessings his family received
solution:
Abundance means an excessive availability of something.

Paucity means fewness in number
subject: English Language

question:
choose the word(s) or phrase(s) which best fill(s) the gap(s).

  The pastor asked the women to _________ their hearts to God in prayers.
solution:
To bare one’s mind means to reveal or uncover all that is in it.

  Note the spelling.
subject: English Language

question:
choose the word(s) or phrase(s) which best fill(s) the gap(s).

  We saw you earlier but we could not stop you because we did not know __________.
solution: The expression is in its past tense and should maintain a past tense all through
subject: English Language

question:
choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) in italics.

  It is customary to find young men and women in sports.
solution:
Customary means habitual or conventional.

Unusual means unlike what is expected.It is the antonym of ‘customary’
subject: English Language

question:
choose the option nearest in meaning to the italicized words or expressions.

  High mortality rate is becoming increasingly predominant.
solution:
‘Predominant’ means common, significant and important.

‘Rampant’ means common and unrestrained.
subject: English Language

question:
choose the option nearest in meaning to the italicized words or expressions.

  The rapist claimed that the woman’s voluptuous hips attracted him
solution: ‘Voluptuous’ means full and attractive.
subject: English Language

question:
Select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

  Grandma told the boys a story that cheered them up.
solution:
To cheer one up means to make them happy especially after having been sad.

To be delighted means to be happy. The correct answer is C.
subject: English Language

question:
choose the option that best completes the gap.

  The cliff is popularly known for ___________ morning beauty.
solution: ‘‘Its’’ is the correct possessive form.
subject: English Language

question:
select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

  The team adapted to the new tactic as clean as a whistle.
solution: The expression ‘as clean as a whistle’ means perfectly and without errors.

options.a: They adapted to the pattern perfectly
options.b: They needed a whistle to be able to adapt to the pattern.
options.c: They adapted to the tactic with little errors
options.d: They found it difficult to adapt to the new tactic.

subject: English Language

question:
choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) in italics.

  The driver was discharged while the car owner was ___________ for fraud.
solution:
To discharge someone means to legally release them from confinement.

To remand means to be charged and awaiting trials.
subject: English Language

question:
choose the option nearest in meaning to the italicized words or expressions.

  The police later discovered that Audu feigned death.
solution:
To feign means to make a false show of something.

To fake something means to make a false display.
subject: English Language

question: Aliya keeps having a "C" grade in what subject?
solution: Aliya is a smart and brilliant student who has A grades in all her subjects except chemistry where she has C.
subject: English Language

question: In the novel, Aliya sufferes "body shaming" from which of these charaters?
solution: Her Biology Teacher [See Page 46]
subject: English Language

question: Abdullahi, having written extensively over the years on politics, policy  and development. "Sweeet Sixteen was his" ________________
solution: First book [Read about the novelist; Bolaji Abdullahi]
subject: English Language

question: Which of the following was the concluding story hold by Aliya's father to solidify Aliya's Self-realization?
solution: The Hunter's Story [See Page 51 - 53]

options.c: The Slave and the Captor story

subject: English Language

question: The Novel "Sweet Sixteen" is naratted in ______________
solution: This is a narrative techniques/point of view where the story is narrated by one character at a time. The character may be speaking about himself or sharing events that he or she is expecting.
subject: English Language

question: Which of these character is opinionated that relationships comes with a lot of emotional issues one can bear?
solution: Aliya's Father [See Page 34 - 36]
subject: English Language

question: What was Aliya's reason of not considering choosing a career as a doctor in the novel?
solution: Because she hates the sight of blood

options.a: Because she hates the sight of blood

question:
Choose the one nearest in meaning to the word(s) in bracket

Okibe was rusticated for his [derogatory] remark about the principal
solution:
Derogatory; means showing a critical or disrespectful attitude.

Unsavoury; means unpleasant or morally offensive, the most suitable synonym for derogatory in this regard.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the one nearest in meaning to the word(s) in bracket

Some men will continue to cause offences until they are given [a taste of their own medicine]
solution:
The expression "a taste of their own medicine" means to experience the same harmful or negative treatment that one has inflicted on others. In the context of the sentence, it suggests that some men will continue to cause offences until they themselves experience the consequences or negative actions similar to what they have done to others.

Option B, "revenged on," is the nearest in meaning because it conveys the idea that these men will continue their offensive behaviour until they face retaliation or consequences similar to what they have caused to others.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the one nearest in meaning to the word(s) in bracket

Justice is difficult to enforce because people are unwilling to accept any loss of [sovereignty]
solution: Autonomy is the correct answer. Sovereignty means a situation of self-sufficiency and self-governance.

solution: However is used when trying to include a new point, or buttress an old one with a comparison

question:

question: A suitable title for the above passage can be:
solution: The passage is expository enough to largely cover mass communication as a career, practice, relative importance and societal impact and effect.

options.b: The mass media and the people
options.d: The good and the bad side of the media

section:
Read the Passage carefully and answer the questions that follow.

The term Mass Communication occurs when information is disseminated to a relatively large number of people in different places. it should be seen as the same thing with talking face to face with someone else. With mass communication, there is no answer, smile, laugh or lock of surprise. the people who receive the message are as far away from the source of the message as far away as you are from the announcers on the radio or from the newscasters on television, or from the writer of a column in the local newspaper.
There are three identifiable means of errors in mass communication. There is one done through the printed words, books, magazine and newspapers. The other is primarily radio, but also records and tape recordings. Also, it can be done through a combination of sound and pictures as in television, films and the more recently popular video tape. Together, they are referred to as the mass media.
All media can be used to inform and entertain. However, there are coverts roles played. It may be to educate as in school broadcast. Again, the aim may be to persuade as when the media are used by advertisers or for political broadcasts. The media are often in strong position to influence public opinion because they select the topics to be presented and can stress the importance of one issue over the other.
The mass media have, in recent times, come under acerbic criticism. Many presenters have become not only conscientized but also immensely concerned about the possible effects that science of violence and bad behavior may have on their children. More worrisome are the potential danger of political and commercial propaganda.
subject: English Language

question: The means of mass media can be summarized into:
solution: Mass media means technology that is intended to reach a mass audience. It is the primary means of communication used to reach the vast majority of the general public. The most common platforms for mass media according to the passage are newspapers, magazines, radio, television. These can be summarized into print (Newspaper, magazines) and electronic (Radio and Television)

Again, the aim may be to persuade as when the media are used by advertisers or for political broadcasts. The media are often in strong position to influence public opinion because they select the topics to be presented and can stress the importance of one issue over the other.
The mass media have, in recent times, come under acerbic criticism. Many presenters have become not only conscientized but also immensely concerned about the possible effects that science of violence and bad behavior may have on their children. More worrisome are the potential danger of political and commercial propaganda.
subject: English Language

question: Bridget denied that she wasn't there. This means that
solution:
'Deny'' is to say that something is not true.

'that she wasn't there' is the accusation.

For Bridget to deny that she wasn't there, she is simply saying it is untrue to say she wasn't there.  Therefore, she was there.

options.a: She confessed that she wasn't there
options.b: She owned up not to be there

question: Had it not rained, it wouldn't have been so bad

options.b: it rained and so it wasn't bad
options.c: it wasn't bad because it rained
options.d: it rained and that made it so bad

The ACCOUNTANT paid the workers july salary in september

question: Which of the following is the main topic of the passage?
solution: The extract plainly is an exposition to the nature and attributes of sea anemones.

options.d: A comparison of land and sea animals

section:
READ THE PASSAGE CAREFULLY AND ANSWER THE QUESTIONS THAT FOLLOW.

With its radiant color and plant-like shape, the sea anemone looks more like a flower than animal. More specifically, the sea anemone is formed quite like a flower for which is named, with body like a stem and tentacles like petals in brilliant shades; blue, pink, green and red. Its diameters vary from about six millimeters in some species to more than ninety centimeters in the giant verities of Australia. Like corals, hydras and jelly fish, sea anemones are coelenterates. They can move slowly, but more often they attach the lower part of their cylindrical bodies to rocks, shells or wharf pilings.
The upper and end of the sea anemone has a mouth surrounded by tentacles which the animal uses to capture its food. Stinging cells in the tentacles throw out tiny poison threads that paralyze other small sea animals. The tentacles then drag this prey into the sea anemone's mouth.The food is digested in the large inner body cavity. When disturbed, a sea anemone retracts its lump on a rock. Anemones may reproduce by forming eggs. dividing in half or developing buds that grow and break off as independent animals.
subject: English Language

solution: The plant-like of the sea anemone is descriptive of its form or structure.

solution: Option A is the best answer. Note that 'either' goes with 'or' and the usage of both creates an impression as to alternatives.

solution: 'It is high time' suggests that something should be done and that it is already a bit late.

question: You can have ........ of these two books
solution: 'Either' is used when there is an alternative choice or decision to be made.

question: Either Buntu or you ........ to go
solution:
The rule of concord states an exception, that is when either or neither joins to subjects (one singular and the other plural)  the verb should be chosen by considering the nearer of the two subjects.

This means that if the second subject is plural the verb should be plural

'You' can take a plural form, therefore option B is the safest answer.

question: Theatre was popular in Africa because__________
solution:
In the passage, paragraph one emphasizes on community participation, peace and progress. 

Option D is the correct answer

options.a: was traditional and simple in nature
options.b: dealt with the political, social, and economic problems of the society
options.d: was communal and reflected the common concerns of the people

section:
Theatre in the recent past used to be a very popular art in traditional African society. It used to be a point of intersection where members of the community not only come to entertain themselves, 'but join heads together'. In the traditional context of African drama, therefore, theatre was popular and respectable institution which preserved the people's culture and tradition. Theatre was popular with the people because it emphasized community participation, peace and progress. The presentations focused on the people's lives, their aspirations, fears, and hopes. But today, the situation is different. Theatre is becoming very unpopular.

 

Africa of the present age is pre-occupied with many problems yearning for immediate solutions. The continent is facing hydra-head challenges - challenges on the political, social, and economic scenes. In a world where Science and Technology are seen as the solutions to these problems, little attention is paid to the arts. Literature generally, and drama in particular is often rated very low on the utility-scale. Many Africa today look at drama and theatre as a mere thing of fun, a joke so to say.

 

Elitism is another barrier that militates against the appreciation of theatre as a communal art. Folk theatre is appreciated by a negligible number of people, contemporary focus is on literary theatre. Unfortunately, literary theatre only pretends to serve the interest of its society while in reality, it has a foreign audience in mind. The use of European and American theatrical conventions by our academic playwrights can bear witness to this anomaly.

 

The popularity of the literary African theatre is further marred by the medium of communication as most literary dramas in Africa are written in foreign languages which are not understood by many Africans. The question often asked is whether the artist should climb down to the level of his community of stay at his exalted height and wait for the community to gradually move up to him.
subject: English Language

question: One of the reasons why theatre is unpopular in modern Africa is that it_________
solution:

Option A is the correct answer

options.a: is regarded as not being of much use
options.b: only deals with culture and tradition
options.d: is always funny and looked down upon

question: It is important for university to be independent so that they can________
solution:
In the passage, Paragraph one talks about the need for Universities to maintain a level of independence which enables Universities to thrive for intellectual pursuit. The writer also emphasizes the need for University autonomy. All these points out to the need to expand and function independently.

Option C is the correct answer.

options.c: be left to expand without any hindrances

section:
The financing of the university will always be a vexatious problem not only because of the need for it to maintain a level of independence that allows for the thriving of intellectual pursuit but also for the size of the funding that is required to maintain an acceptable level of infrastructure in a modern university. The experience of the Nigerian university in recent years has, in this regard, been almost alarming. The funding levels requited constitute sufficient reasons for diversifying the sources. Even if the government was in a position on its own to meet all of the university's financial requirements, I consider it desirable that every effort be made to reduce such contribution in the interest of university autonomy. I also hold the view that the university should seek to become dependent, in a substantial way, on funds generated from within. In other words, the sources of funding the university should be diversified as much as possible. Based on this principle, ·we can identify four sources - government, industry, students, and individual philanthropists. I must add quickly that each source has its own set of problems in its continuous interaction with the university, which ought to be addressed here.

 

Of all the four major sources of financing university education mentioned above, contribution through fees from the students involves intricate issues of special concern If government gives as an expression of its obligation to promote the general welfare of society if industry gives in order to enhance its chances of procurement of needed human information and material resources, as well as enhance its social responsibility if the general public gives as an indication of voluntary support for a worthy social cause, students (and by inference, their parents) should give in order to purchase an economic good for meeting their own felt need in the expectation that they will be satisfied with the product purchased.

[Adapted from Onosode, GO: 'The politics of Managing People, Prospects and Poverty: The Role of the University, 1990 OAU Convocation}
subject: English Language

Option A is the correct answer.

options.d: maintain acceptable levels of infrastructural development

In other words, the sources of funding the university should be diversified as much as possible. Based on this principle, ·we can identify four sources - government, industry, students, and individual philanthropists. I must add quickly that each source has its own set of problems in its continuous interaction with the university, which ought to be addressed here.

 

Of all the four major sources of financing university education mentioned above, contribution through fees from the students involves intricate issues of special concern If government gives as an expression of its obligation to promote the general welfare of society if industry gives in order to enhance its chances of procurement of needed human information and material resources, as well as enhance its social responsibility if the general public gives as an indication of voluntary support for a worthy social cause, students (and by inference, their parents) should give in order to purchase an economic good for meeting their own felt need in the expectation that they will be satisfied with the product purchased.

[Adapted from Onosode, GO: 'The politics of Managing People, Prospects and Poverty: The Role of the University, 1990 OAU Convocation}

Calculator

For nouns, the stress is usually on the first syllable except if there is a weak vowel ( /ə/ or /ɪ/) on the first syllable, then it moves to the next one. Since calculator ( /ˈkæl.kjə.leɪ.tər/) does not have a weak vowel in the first syllable, the stress is placed there.

question:
From the words lettered A to D, choose the word or group of words that best completes each of the following sentences.

 

The man, as well as the woman ________ arrested
solution:
In the rule of concord, 'when words such as, as well as, alongside, together with, no less than etc are used, the verb that follows would be the same with the subject of the phrase or clause, that is, the noun or the pronoun that comes before the marker of accompaniment.

'The man' is a singular noun hence a singular verb is needed.

Note that 'arrested' can function as past tense or past participle but in this context, it is a past participle.

However, 'is' is a singular verb used in the present tense, 'has' is a singular used in the perfect tense, 'have' is a singular and plural verb used in the perfect tense and 'been' is used in the perfect tense.

Therefore, option B is the correct answer.
subject: English Language

question:
From the words lettered A to D, choose the word or group of words that best completes each of the following sentences.

 

Many a ____________ to understand me
solution:
In the rule of Concord, 'when many-a is used, the verb and the noun that follows must be singular'

Option B is the correct answer.
subject: English Language

question:
From the words lettered A to D, choose the word or group of words that best completes each of the following sentences.

 

It is you that __________ to write the letter
solution:
The law of proximity states that any subject closest to the verb element controls the verb form.

The sentence has a singular subject 'it' and a plural subject 'you', hence a plural verb is needed because 'you' is closer.

Similarly, 'supposed' is most appropriate because of the relative clause 'that'. This implies that supposed functions as an 'adjective' in this context which means 'intended'

Option D is the correct answer.
subject: English Language

question:
From the words lettered A to D, choose the word or group of words that best completes each of the following sentences.

 

My father _______ on the floor
solution:
'My Father' as the subject is in the third person category, should be followed by the auxiliary verb 'has' 

Think of the verb 'lie' as a word with two different meaning.

The first 'lie' means 'to rest in a horizontal position' The following are its past, present participle or past participle tense respectively, (lay, lying , lain)

The second lie means 'to give false information' The following are its past, present participle or past participle tense respectively, (lied, lying, lied)

Contextually and grammatically option D is the correct answer.
subject: English Language

question:

solution:
Comprise means 'made up of'. Therefore, to say 'Comprises of/ Comprise of ' is a tautology.

Note that, singular verbs must agree with a singular noun.

The subject of the sentence is 'This Class' which is a singular noun

This makes Option B correct.

question: A suitable title for the passage is
solution: Option B is the answer to the question. The passage describes fatigue and how it operates in the human body.
section:
Read the passages below and answer the questions that follow:

 Everyone is tired or fatigued at some time. The major cycle in your life is work, fatigue and rest in that order. Fatigue is characteristic of your body. It does not occur in a man-made machine which operates as long as its parts are intact and it has fuel. But your body, a living machine, has a definite limitation - its work continues, it gradually loses its responsiveness, becomes less irritable, turns out less work and finally may not respond at all. The feeling of fatigue usually expresses itself in three ways: first, there is a feeling of tiredness and a marked desire for rest. Second, efficiency is greatly reduced. Third, there may be definite physiological changes in your body, low blood pressure, loss of muscle tone, tremors, and poor muscular coordination, and in other ways. Fatigue, however, may express itself in many ways for there are many different forms of it. The fatigue of a student. for example, who has worked all evening on a difficult lesson, is different from that of a labourer who has worked all day at a back-breaking task, or that of a business executive who worries with the stress and strain of organisation.
subject: English Language

question: According to the passage, the worst stage of fatigue is
solution: The third stage of fatigue is the worst stage and Poor muscular condition can be identified with the third stage.
section:
Read the passages below and answer the questions that follow:

 Everyone is tired or fatigued at some time. The major cycle in your life is work, fatigue and rest in that order. Fatigue is characteristic of your body. It does not occur in a man-made machine which operates as long as its parts are intact and it has fuel. But your body, a living machine, has a definite limitation - its work continues, it gradually loses its responsiveness, becomes less irritable, turns out less work and finally may not respond at all. The feeling of fatigue usually expresses itself in three ways: first, there is a feeling of tiredness and a marked desire for rest. Second, efficiency is greatly reduced. Third, there may be definite physiological changes in your body, low blood pressure, loss of muscle tone, tremors, and poor muscular coordination, and in other ways. Fatigue, however, may express itself in many ways for there are many different forms of it. The fatigue of a student. for example, who has worked all evening on a difficult lesson, is different from that of a labourer who has worked all day at a back-breaking task, or that of a business executive who worries with the stress and strain of organisation.
subject: English Language

question: It can be inferred from the passage that
solution: According to the passage, the concluding part informs us that fatigue can take various forms, distinctively among students, labourers or business executives. These different activities come with different forms of fatigue.

options.a: every task can eventually result in fatigue
options.b: only difficult lessons lead to fatigue
options.c: a labourer must work all day before expressing fatigue
options.d: rest is needed only when you experience fatigue

question: A suitable title for the passage is
solution: The passage is about the Nigerian Educational sector with close attention to the incompetence of the use of English language noticeable among the present Nigerians.
section: It is normal in Nigeria to use proficiency in the use of English language as a barometer for evaluating the height of any person's educational attainment. Today, many teachers resort quite often to the use of vernacular in the teaching of their subjects. One is often dismayed at the incompetence of many teachers, even graduates in the use of English language. Throughout the colonial period, up to 1960, Nigerian formal education was patterned after the English system. The "accent" was on English and an educated Nigerian was one who was only African in colour but English in thought and culture. The ability to speak English fluently and if possible with an Oxford accent was the hallmark of excellence even if the speaker was empty of thought and ideas. In those days it was a serious offence for a secondary school boy or girl to "laugh in the vernacular". But we now swung to the other extreme. Nigeria would say they are not English people, some would say they did not specialise in English in the university. But the country has adopted the English language as its official, the language of instruction at schools, parliamentary proceedings, conduct of official business and indeed the lingua franca. If the language is to continue to be used,  then it is imperative that we all agree on the pattern of correctness in the language so that communication can be effective.
subject: English Language

question: Which of the following is not true according to the passage?
solution: Option C is the only false statement amongst others. according to the passage, the writer advocates for the use of the English Language.

options.a: the competence level in the use of English has fallen greatly
options.b: what used to be in the past could be considered extreme
options.c: the writer is advocating for the use of indigenous language for teaching and learning
options.d: English language is the official language in Nigeria

section:
In the passage below, the numbered gaps (11-20) indicate missing words. From the options lettered A- E the most appropriate option.

The head of the -11- is called the queen bee. She is -12-than the rest of the bees. Her main task in the colony is to lay eggs. Most of the other bees are the -13- bees. These bees collect nectar and -14- from flowers. The nectar that is carried by the worker bees is deposited on the hive and then -15- into honey. The worker bees also help to look after the young bees. As soon as they are -16-, the worker bees feed the young bees with pollen and nectar. This new queen will eventually take over the duties of the old queen when the -20- dies.

question:
choose the most appropriate option nearest in meaning to the word(s) underlined.

He maintained that Nigerians need a leader with an iron fist
solution: Iron fist is used in reference to the exercise of power in an oppressive or ruthless way. A particularly severe, forceful, and uncompromising manner of ruling/governing.
subject: English Language

question:
choose the most appropriate option nearest in meaning to the word(s) underlined.

The emphasis placed on good moral conduct by the society in the past seems to have been relaxed
solution: relax: make or become less tense or anxious.
subject: English Language

question:
choose the most appropriate option nearest in meaning to the word(s) underlined.

The matter was considered a private concern of the chairman to handle
solution: Private: belonging to or for the use of one particular person or group of people only.
subject: English Language

question:
choose the most appropriate option nearest in meaning to the word(s) underlined. 

He was underrated at the preliminary stage of the competition
solution: Preliminary: preceding or done in preparation for something fuller or more important.
subject: English Language

question:
choose the most appropriate option nearest in meaning to the word(s) underlined.

The office assistant was dismissed for pilfering
solution: PIlfering: steal (things of little value).
subject: English Language

question:
choose the most appropriate option nearest in meaning to the word(s) underlined.
Almost everyone she had worked with took advantage of her meekness
solution: Meekness: the fact or condition of being meek; submissiveness.
subject: English Language

question:
choose the most appropriate option nearest in meaning to the word(s) underlined.

Barely six months after his father's demise, he has nothing left again of his patrimony
solution: Patrimony: property inherited from one's father or male ancestor. Also, valued things passed down from previous generations.
subject: English Language

question:
choose the most appropriate option nearest in meaning to the word(s) underlined.

His grandfather was one of the few patriotic Nigerians who ever lived.
solution: Patriotic: having or expressing devotion to and vigorous support for one's country. It also implies showing love for your country and being proud of it
subject: English Language

question:
choose the most appropriate option nearest in meaning to the word(s) underlined.

Many people believed that such an action is unbecoming of a leader
solution: Unbecoming: (of behaviour) not fitting or appropriate; unseemly.
subject: English Language

question:
choose the most appropriate option nearest in meaning to the word(s) underlined.

The governor was incoherent in his speech
solution: Incoherent: (of spoken or written language) expressed in an incomprehensible or confusing way; unclear.
subject: English Language

question:
choose the option nearly opposite in meaning to the word(s) underlined

The lady has devised a means of tackling her current challenges.
solution:
Tackling: make determined efforts to deal with (a problem or difficult task).

Avoiding: keep away from or stop oneself from doing (something).
subject: English Language

question:
choose the option nearly opposite in meaning to the word(s) underlined

One hundred and forty kilometres per hour is a reasonable speed limit.
solution:
Reasonable:as much as is appropriate or fair; moderate.

Outrageous: shockingly bad or excessive.
subject: English Language

question:
choose the option nearly opposite in meaning to the word(s) underlined

The annual festival attracts dignitaries from both home and abroad.
solution:
Dignitary: a person considered to be important because of high rank or office.

nonentity: a person or thing with no special or interesting qualities; an unimportant person or thing.
subject: English Language

question:
choose the option nearly opposite in meaning to the word(s) underlined

The soldiers were remorseful for their inhuman treatment of the civilians.
solution:
Remorseful: filled with remorse; sorry.

Boastful: showing excessive pride and self-satisfaction in one's achievements, possessions, or abilities.
subject: English Language

question:
choose the option nearly opposite in meaning to the word(s) underlined

Insecurity has escalated since the present government came into power.
solution:
Escalate: increase rapidly.

Abate: (of something unpleasant or severe) become less intense or widespread
subject: English Language

question:
choose the option nearly opposite in meaning to the word(s) underlined

It is embarrassing to accept our mistakes and tender an apology.
solution:
Embarrassing: causing embarrassment.

Honourable: bringing or deserving honour.
subject: English Language

question:
choose the option nearly opposite in meaning to the word(s) underlined

The lawmakers are perceived to be the greediest set of politicians.
solution:
Greedy: Having or showing a strong or excessive desire to acquire money or possess things,

Selfless: concerned more with the needs and wishes of others than with one's own; unselfish.
subject: English Language

question:
choose the option nearly opposite in meaning to the word(s) underlined

James found the photocopied material invaluable while preparing for the examination
solution: The word "invaluable" in the context of the sentence means extremely useful or of great value. The opposite of "invaluable" is something without value or usefulness. Therefore, "worthless" (option C) is the choice that is nearly opposite in meaning to "invaluable." If something is "worthless," it has little or no value.
subject: English Language

question:
choose the option nearly opposite in meaning to the word(s) underlined

Mr. Akin's health has greatly degenerated in the last week.
solution:
Degenerated: decline or deteriorate physically, mentally, or morally.

improved: having become or been made better.
subject: English Language

question:
choose the option nearly opposite in meaning to the word(s) underlined

The incumbent president conceded defeat before the final result was announced.
solution:
Concede: admit or agree that something is true after first denying or resisting it.

Reject: refuse to agree to (a request).

question: Early in which month did the southwest moonsoon break?
section:
May in Ayemenem is a hot, brooding month. The days are long and humid. The river shrinks and black crows gorge on bright mangoes in still, dust green trees. Red bananas ripen. Jackfruits burst. Dissolute blue bottles hum vacuously in the fruity air. Then they stun themselves against clear windowpanes and die, fatly baffled in the sun. The nights are clear but suffused with sloth and sullen expectations.

But by early June the southwest monsoon breaks and there are three months of wind and water with short spells of sharp, glittering sunshine that thrilled children snatch to play with. The countryside turns an immodest green. Boundaries blur as tapioca fences take root and bloom. Brick walls turn mossgreen. Pepper vines snake up electric poles. Wild creepers burst through laterite banks and spilt across the flooded roads. Boats ply in the bazaars. And small fish appear in the puddles that fill the PWD potholes on the highways. It was raining when Rahel came
back to Ayemenem.

Slanting silver ropes slammed into loose earth, ploughing it up like gunfire. The old house on the hill wore its steep, gabled roof pulled over its ears like a low hat. The walls, streaked with moss, had grown soft and bulged a little with dampness that seeped up from the ground. The wild, overgrown garden was full of the whisper and scurry of small lives.In the undergrowth, a rat snake rubbed itself against a glistening stone. Hopeful yellow bullfrogs cruised the scummy pond for mates. A drenched mongoose flashed across the leaf-strewn driveway. The house itself looked empty. The doors and windows were locked. The front verandah bare. Unfurnished.

But the sky blue Plymouth with chrome tail fins was still parked outside, and inside, Baby Kochamma was still alive. She was Rahel's baby grand aunt, her grandfather's younger sister. Her name was really Navomi, Navomi Ipe, but everybody called her Baby. She became Baby Kochamma when she was old enough to be an aunt. Rahel hadn't come to see her, though.
Neither niece nor baby grandaunt laboured under any illusions on that account. Rahel had come to see her brother, Estha. They were two-egg twins. "Dizygotic' doctors called them. Born from separate but simultaneously fertilized eggs. Estha Esthappen-was the older by 18 minutes.
subject: English Language

question: When did Christopher Marlowe get his first degree?
solution: Self-explanatory.
section:
Read the following passage and answer the questions below:

Christopher Marlowe, the son of Marlowe who is described as the clerk of St Mary's in the city of Canterbury, was born in 1564. He received his early education at The King's School in that city. He took the degrees of B.A. and M.A. in 1583 and 1587 respectively from Cambridge University. He started writing poems and became a renowned dramatist in the Admiral's Company.

Marlowe is known as one of the university wits, a group of dramatists along with Robert Greene, Thomas Nash, and George Peele. Marlowe is considered the greatest precursor to Shakespeare. When Marlowe came upon the English stage, the nature of drama was undeveloped. The verses in the dramas were lifeless. But Marlowe gave English drama an appropriate meter, diction, and method. It is really a matter of speculation what kind of greatness he would have achieved if his life had not been terminated (C++) in a duel after a brawl in a tavern (inn).

The literary life of Marlowe had a short span, from 1587 to 1593. During this period, he wrote five plays which were all tragedies as he had no comic vein. His tragedies like Dr Faustus, The Jew of Malta, and Edward Il are rated as some of the best works of the Elizabethan age. He also wrote an unfinished poem Hero and Leander and translated some parts of Ovid's (a Roman Poet) elegies.

Marlowe's subjects are mostly heroic which appeals to the imagination of the play-goers. His heroes epitomize the spirit of the Renaissance. Each of them embodies a passion and tries to achieve lies in his development of the blank verse. He put it at any cost. But Marlowe's chief contribution aside from the old rhyming lines then employed in the plays and used blank verse. Thus, the language of drama was brought closer to real life and drama was made ready for Shakespeare (1564-1616) to improve upon it. It is rightly said, "No Marlowe, no Shakespeare".
subject: English Language

solution: When two subjects are joined together by "and" but the two subjects refer to only one person or thing, a singular verb should be used.

solution: When there is a list of nouns or pronouns at the level of the subject, it is the nearest noun or pronoun to the position of the verb that will determine the choice of the verb.

solution:
battle - /ˈbæt.əl/

raffle - /'ræf.əl/

latter - /'læt.ə/

bitter - /ˈbɪt.ə/

kettle - /'ket.əl/

Battle and kettle have the same rhyme scheme because they both end with the syllable /təl/.
subject: English Language

question:
The words in capital letter have the emphatic stress. Choose the options to which the given sentences relate.
I bought my WIFE a red dress
solution:
Emphatic stress is the placement of greater stress on a particular word in a phrase or sentence. In this case, the word wife is emphasized to correct a mistake that has been made in the question. To find the appropriate question to the response: I bought my WIFE a new dress, you have to find the question that has a word that is different from the emphasized word/ the word in capital letters. The correct question is

A. Did I buy my husband a new dress?

No. I bought my WIFE a new dress.

options.a: Did I buy my husband a red dress?
options.b: Did I buy my wife a red shoe?
options.c: Did he buy my wife a red dress?
options.d: Did I buy my wife a black dress?

subject: English Language

question:
This question is based on "The Life Changer" novel.

Dr. Samuel Johnson is also known as_____
solution: He is also known as Samjohn. We can find this in pg. 19 of the novel where Ummi's husband casually refers to Dr Samuel Johnson as Dr. Samjohn, his friend who had helped with Ummi's admission.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the word that has the same CONSONANT SOUND as the one represented by the letter(s) underlined.
wash
solution:
wash - /ʃ/

treasure - /ʒ/

occasion - /ʒ/

nation - /ʃ/

equation - /ʒ/
subject: English Language

question:
In each of the following sentences, the word that resolves the emphatic stress is written in capital letters. From the options letters A to D, choose the one to which the given sentence is the appropriate answer.

Daddy plays TENNIS on Saturday afternoons
solution:
Emphatic stress is the placement of greater stress on a particular word in a phrase or sentence. In this case, the word wife is emphasized to correct a mistake that has been made in the question. To find the appropriate question to the response: Daddy plays TENNIS on Saturday afternoons, you have to find the question that has a word that is different from the emphasized word/ the word in capital letters. The correct question is:

D. Does Daddy play cricket on Saturday afternoon?

No. Daddy plays TENNIS on Saturday afternoon.

options.a: Does mummy play tennis on Saturday afternoons?
options.b: Does daddy play tennis on Sunday afternoons?
options.c: Does daddy play tennis on Saturday morning?
options.d: Does daddy play cricket on Saturday afternoon?

subject: English Language

question:
In this question, select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

If he were here, it could be more fun.
solution: The correct answer is 'He did not show up and so the occasion lacked much fun'. The sentence 'If he were here, it could be more fun' is a hypothetical condition that implies that the person being referred to is not present, and his presence could have made the situation more enjoyable. The other options do not fully capture the essence of the sentence.

options.a: He did not show up and so the occasion lacked much fun
options.b: He was expected but did not show up to liven up the occasion
options.c: There was no fun because he was not present
options.d: He was being expected to supply more fun

subject: English Language

question:
In this question, select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

Though he is our elected representative, he often takes a rather jaundiced view of our problems.
solution: The phrase 'jaundiced view' is an idiomatic expression that means having a cynical or sceptical view of something. It does not mean hazy, forceful or sickly. Therefore, the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence is 'He takes an unfavourable position concerning our problems'.

options.a: He takes a rather hazy view of our problems
options.b: He takes an unfavourable position concerning our problems
options.c: He takes a rather forceful view of our problems
options.d: He takes a sickly view of our problems

subject: English Language

question:
From the words lettered A to D, choose the word that has the same consonant sound(s) as the one represented by the letter(s) underlined below.

Pin
solution: The consonant sound represented by 'P' in 'Pin' is the same as in 'Pragmatic'. In English phonetics, the 'P' in 'Pin' and 'Pragmatic' is a voiceless bilabial plosive, represented as /p/.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the appropriate stress pattern from the options.The stressed words are in capital letter(s)
Fluency
solution: In English pronunciation, stress is placed on one syllable in a word. The word 'Fluency' is pronounced with stress on the first syllable, therefore the correct stress pattern is 'FLU-en-cy'.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the word that is opposite in meaning to the underlined word(s)
Victor played the role of a haughty prince.
solution: Haughty means arrogantly superior and disdainful. Its antonyms include modest, humble, unpretentious, unassuming, low-key and unostentatious.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the word or group of words that is most nearly opposite in meaning to the underlined words
The gradual process of transition to civilian rule came to an _____ end.
solution: The word 'gradual' means taking place or progressing slowly or by degrees. Therefore, the word 'abrupt' which means sudden and unexpected is the opposite of 'gradual'.
subject: English Language

question:
Fill in the gap in the sentence below with the appropriate option:
Peter and Jane love each other ...... the difficulties they face.
solution: "despite" is used to join two contrasting situations in a sentence. e.g He was rich despite the difficulties he went through.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word
I was skeptical about the story she told me.
solution: The word 'skeptical' means not easily convinced; or having doubts or reservations. Therefore, the nearest meaning to 'skeptical' among the options is 'doubtful'.
subject: English Language

question:
This question is based on "The Life Changer" novel.

_________ is the euphemism use for Cheat notes.
solution: In the context of the novel 'The Life Changer', the euphemism used for cheat notes is 'Notes of salvation'.This can be found on page 52 of the novel where Salma convinced her seat partner, Kola, during her final examination to scribble and slip her these "notes of salvation" as she had nothing to write.
subject: English Language

question:
This question is based on "The Life Changer" novel.

why did Omar say he passed his SSCE by no means a small feat?
solution: In the novel 'The Life Changer', Omar says he passed his SSCE by no means a small feat because he passed excellently on his first attempt. This can be found on page 8 of the novel. He was bragging to his sister, Teemah, about how he passed his exams excellently even when some of his colleagues paid a lot of money to some 'Miracle Centres' and still failed their exams.

options.b: because he failed other subjects but passed maths and English
options.c: because he registered with one of the miracle centers
options.d: Because he passed excellently on his first attempt

subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that has the same vowel sound as the one represented by the letter[s] underlined.

naked
solution:
The correct answer is bid.

The 'e' in the word 'naked' is pronounced as  /ɪ/ - /ˈneɪkɪd/. Therefore the word with the same vowel sound is 'bid'.
subject: English Language

question:
From the list of words in A to D, choose one that best describes the sentence.
James and Henry were at daggers drawn when I knew them. This means that they
solution: The phrase 'at daggers drawn' is an idiom which means to be bitterly hostile or at enmity with someone. It does not literally mean that they always wore daggers or were good at drawing daggers or were fighting everybody. Therefore, the option 'were always angry with each other' best describes the sentence.

options.a: were always angry with each other

subject: English Language

question:
This question is based on "The Life Changer" novel.

Who among Salma's roommate was reserved and withdrawn yet generous to a fault?
solution: In the novel 'The Life Changer', Ngozi is described as being reserved and withdrawn yet generous to a fault. It is her generosity that leads to a strong bond between the roommates. She always shares her food with the rest and this leads them to start cooking as a small family.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the word that has the same rhyme scheme as the word below:
Day
solution:
Rhyme scheme refers to the pattern of end sounds or words in a verse or line of poetry. In this case, we are looking for a word that rhymes with 'Day' /deɪ/

dare -  /deə/

anime  - /ˈænɪmeɪ/

flight - /flaɪt/

ball - /bɔːl/

The word day and anime end with the /eɪ/ sound, therefore, they rhyme with each other.
subject: English Language

question:
In this question, fill each gap with the most appropriate option from the list provided.

Without our relentless campaigns, we might ...... sure of victory in the race for the National Assembly.
solution: never have been is most appropriate. Because of the use of the modal "might," the form of anomalous HAVE to use is have. Then, after "have" comes the past participle "been".
subject: English Language

question:
In this question, fill each gap with the most appropriate option from the list provided.

We are all hungry; we ...... anything to eat since morning.
solution: haven't had is most appropriate. This is known as the present perfect tense which is used to show that an action has been completed (or not) as of the time of speaking.
subject: English Language

question:
In this question, fill each gap with the most appropriate option from the list provided.

To check desertification in the arid zones, ...... trees should be planted
solution: drought-resistant is most appropriate. Drought tolerance refers to the degree to which a plant is adapted to arid or drought conditions.
subject: English Language

question:
From the options lettered A-D, choose the option that is most nearly opposite in meaning to the underlined word.

My father is parsimonious.
solution: Parsimonious means to be very unwilling to spend money or use resources. The nearly opposite word is generous.
subject: English Language

question: Pick out the option with a different rhyme
solution: The word 'Growl' has a different rhyme from the other words. Rhyming words are words that have the same ending sound. In this case, 'Bore', 'Four', and 'Roar' all end with the '-ore' sound, while 'Growl' ends with the '-owl' sound.
subject: English Language

question:
The primary stress is indicated by writing the syllable on which it occurs in capital letters. From the words lettered A to D choose the one that has the correct stress.
category
solution: In English pronunciation, the word 'category' has the primary stress on the first syllable. Therefore, the correct option is 'CAT-e-go-ry'.
subject: English Language

question:
This question is based on "The Life Changer" novel.

Ummi is an Arabic word that is directly translated to mean_____
solution: In Arabic, 'Ummi' is directly translated to mean 'My mother'. This can be seen on page 3 of the novel. According to Ummi, she was named after her paternal grandmother.
subject: English Language

question:
From the words lettered A to D below, choose the word or group of words that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word as it is used in the passage.

Bisi has become an indispensable member of staff.
solution: The word 'indispensable' means something or someone that is absolutely necessary or extremely important in a particular situation. In this context, it means that Bisi is a very important member of the staff. Therefore, the word 'vital' is the closest in meaning to 'indispensable'.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that has the same vowel sound as the letter(s) underlined

exist
solution:
exist - /ɪgzɪst/
helicopter - /helɪkɒptə/
isle - /aɪl/
meant - /ment/
never - /nevə/
subject: English Language

question:
This question is based on "The Life Changer" novel.

In Lafayette, before a stranger is hosted or accommodated, permission must be requested and granted by_______
solution: In the novel 'The Life Changer', in Lafayette, before a stranger is hosted or accommodated, permission must be requested and granted by the Hakimi. The Hakimi is a traditional title for a local or tribal leader in some areas of the Middle East and North Africa. This can be seen on page 20 of the novel. It is an important tradition that helps to ensure that no criminal or fugitive from justice is harboured or accommodated in the village.
subject: English Language

question: Choose the word that does not have the same vowel sound as the others
solution: The words 'sun', 'shun', and 'son' have the same vowel sound /ʌ/, while 'short' has a different vowel sound /ɔ:/. Therefore, 'short' does not have the same vowel sound as the others.
subject: English Language

question: Athens had _________ the Greek-states against Persians.
solution: From the passage, it is clear that Athens had interceded for the other Greek city-states against the Persian domination. This means that Athens had intervened on behalf of the other Greek city-states. Therefore, the correct answer is option 2.
section:
Use the passage to answer the questions below
Read the passage carefully and answer the questions that follow:

The victory of the small Greek democracy of Athens over the mighty Persian Empire was furious because Athens had interceded for the other Greek city-states in revolt against Persian domination. In anger, the king sent an enormous army to defeat Athens. He thought it would take drastic steps to pacify the rebellious part of the empire.

Persia was ruled by one man. In Athens, however, all citiens helped to rule. Emboldened by this participation, Athenians were prepared to die for their city-state. Perhaps this was the secret of the remarkable victory at the marathon, the Persians tried to fool some Greek city-states by claiming to have come in peace. The frightened city of Delos refused to believe this. Not wanting to abet the conquest of Greece, they fled from their city and did not return until the Persians had left. They were wise, for the president next conquered the city of Eritrea and captured its people.

Tiny Athens stood alone against Persia. The Athenian people to their sanctuaries. There they prayed for deliverance. They asked their gods to expedite their victory. The Athenians refurbished their weapons and moved to the plan of Marathon where their little band would meet the Persians. At the last moment, soldiers from Plataea reinforced the Athenian troops.

The Athenian army attacked and Greek citizens fought bravely. The power of the mighty Persians was offset by the love that the Athenians had for their city. Athenians defeated the Persians in both archery and combat. Greek soldiers seized Persian sheep and burned them and the Persians fled in terror. Herodotus, a famous historian, reports that 6400 Persians died, compared to only 192 Athenians.
subject: English Language

question: Darius took drastic steps to __________ the rebellious Athenians.
solution: In the passage, it is mentioned that 'In anger the king sent an enormous army to defeat Athens. He thought it would take drastic steps to pacify the rebellious part of the empire.' This implies that Darius, the king, took drastic steps to calm the rebellious Athenians. Therefore, the correct answer is option 3.

question: The people of Delos did not want to __________ the conquest of Greece
solution: In the passage, it is mentioned that 'The frightened city of Delos refused to believe this. This suggests that the people of Delos did not want to encourage or support the conquest of Greece by the Persians. Therefore, the correct answer is 'encourage'.

question:
This question is based on "The Life Changer" novel.

Salma was looking more stunning on the last day of her exams because_______
solution: Self-explanatory

options.a: her classmates had planned to go to the beach after the exam
options.b: her classmates had organized a mini picnic to wrap up the good work
options.d: her classmates had planned a photo shoot with some lecturers

subject: English Language

question:
This question is based on "The Life Changer" novel.

Why was it a double celebration for Ummi and members of her community when she gained admission into the university?
solution: Self-explanatory

options.b: She had given birth to Omar before her admission to the university
options.c: Her husband agreed to marry her before she went for university registration
options.d: she was the first child to leave Lafayette to the university

question: ...1...
solution: This best suits because the senate has to do with the legislative arm and they are saddled with responsibility of law making.
section:
The passage below has gaps. Immediately following each gap, four options are provided. Choose the most appropriate option for each gap.



PASSAGE IV

With the most profound respect to the members of the Senate, I do not think that it is within the competence of that ...1... body to pass a motion to ...2... the executive action of the President. The Senate is ...3... of the National Assembly. But it is not by itself alone the National Assembly. One can imagine the confusion, which would be created if the ...4... were to take a view diametrically opposed to that reflected in the Senate resolution. The strongest objection to the action of the Senate in passing the resolution is the fact that it constituted itself the ...5... as well as the judge of the constitutionality of the action of the President. The function of the Senate is to ...6... laws. But the Senate has no authority or ...7... to control the President in the exercise of his ...8... powers. It cannot by a mere resolution or motion give any directive to the President regarding the exercise of his powers nor can it undo what the President has done in the exercise of those powers. The only way in which the exercise of the powers of the President can be ...9... is by ...10... of the National Assembly.
Adapted from The Guardian of July 8, 1999, p. 8.
subject: English Language

question: ...2...
solution: The context of the passage suggests that the Senate is trying to 'nullify' the executive action of the President. 'Nullify' means to make legally null and void; invalidate. The Senate, being a legislative body, does not have the power to enforce, modify, or order the executive action of the President.

question: ...3...
solution: In political terminology, an "arm" is often used to refer to a branch or division within a legislative body. The National Assembly is a larger legislative body, and the Senate is a component or division within it.

question: ...4...
solution: In the context of the passage, the author is discussing the potential confusion that could arise if another part of the National Assembly were to disagree with the Senate. The House of Representatives is another part of the National Assembly, making it the correct answer. The Judiciary and the Executive Council are separate branches of government and do not fit the context of the sentence.

question: ...5...
solution: In the context of the passage, the Senate is being criticized for overstepping its bounds and acting as both the 'litigant' and the judge of the constitutionality of the President's actions. A 'litigant' is a party involved in a lawsuit, which fits the context better than the other options. 'Defendant' and 'plaintiff' are specific types of litigants, but the passage does not specify which role the Senate is taking on. 'Attorney' does not fit because the Senate is not representing someone in a legal matter, but rather taking on the role of a party in a lawsuit.
