subject: English Language

question: ...6...
solution: In the context of the passage, the function of the Senate is to 'enact' laws. To 'enact' a law means to make it official or put it into action, which is the role of legislative bodies like the Senate. 'Create' could also work, but 'enact' is more specific to the process of passing laws. 'Compose' is more commonly used in the context of writing or creating something like a piece of music or literature, not laws. 'Annul' means to declare invalid, which is not a function of the Senate mentioned in the passage.
section:
The passage below has gaps. Immediately following each gap, four options are provided. Choose the most appropriate option for each gap.



PASSAGE IV

With the most profound respect to the members of the Senate, I do not think that it is within the competence of that ...1... body to pass a motion to ...2... the executive action of the President. The Senate is ...3... of the National Assembly. But it is not by itself alone the National Assembly. One can imagine the confusion, which would be created if the ...4... were to take a view diametrically opposed to that reflected in the Senate resolution. The strongest objection to the action of the Senate in passing the resolution is the fact that it constituted itself the ...5... as well as the judge of the constitutionality of the action of the President. The function of the Senate is to ...6... laws. But the Senate has no authority or ...7... to control the President in the exercise of his ...8... powers. It cannot by a mere resolution or motion give any directive to the President regarding the exercise of his powers nor can it undo what the President has done in the exercise of those powers. The only way in which the exercise of the powers of the President can be ...9... is by ...10... of the National Assembly.
Adapted from The Guardian of July 8, 1999, p. 8.
subject: English Language

question: ...7...
solution: In the context of the passage, the Senate does not have the authority or 'power' to control the President in the exercise of his powers. The term 'power' refers to the ability or capacity to do something or act in a particular way. The other options 'might', 'dynamism', and 'strength' do not fit into the context of the sentence.

The other options do not fit into the context of the passage.

question:
Choose the word or phrase from A to D which has its meaning opposite to the underlined word or words in each sentence
She was impertinent until she met her husband.
solution: Impertinent means ' not showing proper respect'. It's antonyms include polite, courteous, respectful, deferential, well bred, well mannered and civil.
subject: English Language

question:
This question is based on "The Life Changer" novel.

According to the novel, which of these characters had a nasty experience on the account of using social media?
solution: Salim had a nasty experience using social media.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word(s)

Repugnant rules in the society should be repealed.
solution: Repugnant means extremely distasteful. Its synonyms include abhorrent, revolting, repulsive, repellent and offensive.
subject: English Language

question:
This question is based on "The Life Changer" novel.

Bint was encouraged to take French at the primary level because _____
solution: In the novel 'The Life Changer', Bint was encouraged to take French at the primary level because her parents believed that language acquisition at an early age is relatively easy. This is based on the concept that younger children have a higher ability to absorb and learn new languages.

options.a: Her parents believed that language acquisition at an early age is relatively easy
options.b: she would want to be a French mistress when she grows up
options.d: it will be useful for her when she travels to France

subject: English Language

question:
Choose the options to which the given sentence relate.

Spain won their last friendly FOOTBALL match
solution: Emphatic stress usually emphasis contrast, thus the right option should contain a word that contrasts with the emphasized word.

options.a: Who won their last friendly football match?
options.b: Did Spain win their last competitive football match
options.c: Did Spain lose their last competitive football match
options.d: Did Spain win their last friendly basketball match?

subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word
The boss is quite inflexible once he has made up his mind.
solution: The word 'inflexible' means unwilling to change or compromise. Therefore, the nearest meaning to 'inflexible' among the options is 'unyielding'.
subject: English Language

question:
From the options lettered A-D, choose the option that is most nearly opposite in meaning to the underlined word.

My mother is a shrewd businesswoman.
solution: A shrewd person is able to understand and judge a situation quickly and to use this understanding to their own advantage. The nearest opposite word is gullible.
subject: English Language

question:
In the following question, choose the option that best completes the sentence.
The guest would have arrived earlier but he was _______ in traffic.
solution: The correct phrase to use is 'held up'. 'Held up' is a phrasal verb that means to be delayed or obstructed. So, 'The guest would have arrived earlier but he was held up in traffic' is the correct sentence.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word(s)
No one would complain if you emulate good behaviour
solution: Emulate means to match or surpass, typically by imitation. Its synonyms include imitate, copy, reproduce, mimic, mirror, echo and follow.
subject: English Language

question: He was both a writer and a politician, but he was better ........ a singer
solution: The correct preposition to use after 'better' in this context is 'as'. When comparing roles or functions, 'as' is used. For example, 'He works better as a manager than as a clerk.' Therefore, the sentence should read 'He was both a writer and a politician, but he was better as a singer.'
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the word that has the same consonant sound as the one in bracket.

Smoo[th]
solution: Brother is the correct answer. The sound present here is pronounceable from the 'TH' in 'these' /ð/ (not /d/ as in 'drug' or /θ/ as in 'thanks').
subject: English Language

question: Identify the option with a different stress pattern
solution: In this case, 'importance', 'contribute', and 'deposit' all have the stress on the second syllable. However, 'reservoir' has a different stress pattern with the stress on the third syllable, making it the correct answer.
subject: English Language

question: In each of the questions, the word in capital letters has the emphatic stress. Choose the option to which the given sentence relates.The union congratulated the ELECTED president.
solution: The correct answer is - Did the union congratulate the ousted president? The option without the emphatic word - ELECTED is the correct answer.

options.a: Did the union reject the elected president?
options.b: Did the union congratulate the elected chairman?
options.c: Did the management congratulate the elected president?
options.d: Did the union congratulate the ousted president?

subject: English Language

question:
This question is based on "The Life Changer" novel.

What appellation was given to Talle for all his amazing show of personality.
solution: He was known as the quiet one.
subject: English Language

question: In each of the questions, the word in capital letters has the emphatic stress. Choose the option to which the given sentence relates.A good description INCLUDES specific nouns.
solution: The correct answer is - Does a good description exclude specific nouns?. The option without the emphatic word - INCLUDES is the correct answer.

options.a: Does a good description exclude specific nouns?
options.b: Does a good description include specific verbs?
options.c: Does a good description include obscure nouns?
options.d: Does a poor description include specific nouns?

subject: English Language

question:
Choose the word which has the same consonant sounds as the underlined letter(s)
pleasure
solution: The underlined letter(s) in the word 'pleasure' is 's'. The consonant sound of 's' in 'pleasure' is /ʒ/ (as in 'vision'). Among the options, the word 'seizure' has the same consonant sound /ʒ/.
subject: English Language

question:
This question is based on "The Life Changer" novel.

Whose reticent nature while growing up earned him the title: "The quiet one".?
solution: Talle was regarded as the quiet one while growing
subject: English Language

question:
In this question, select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

The solution lies in choosing between various negative alternatives.
solution: The sentence 'The solution lies in choosing between various negative alternatives' implies that the solution can be found among several unfavorable or less-than-ideal options. Therefore, the option 'The solution can be found in one of the negative options' is the correct answer.

options.b: The solution lies in choosing between the positive and negative
options.c: The solution can be found in one of the negative options
options.d: The solution is a negative one

subject: English Language

question:
In this question, the word in capital letters has the emphatic stress. Choose the option to which the given sentence relates.

EMEKA finished his homework yesterday.
solution: In the sentence, the word 'Emeka' is stressed. This means the emphasis is on 'who' did the action. Therefore, the question that relates to the sentence is 'Who finished his home work yesterday?'.

options.a: Who finished his home work yesterday?
options.b: When did Emeka finish his home work?
options.c: Did Emeka do his home work yesterday?
options.d: Was Emeka helped to do his home work?

subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word(s)
After thousands of years of existing, elephants still remain extant.
solution: Extant means still in existence. It's synonyms include still existing, in existence, surviving, abiding, enduring and existent.
subject: English Language

question:
This question is based on "The Life Changer" novel.

Salma stayed at the most coveted and famous ______ hostel.
solution: Salma stayed in Queen Amina's hostel
subject: English Language

question:
This question is based on "The Life Changer" novel.

Who called the attention of the district head when it was discovered that Talle buys more than he could consume from the supermarket?
solution: Self-explanatory
subject: English Language

question: In this question, identify the word that has the stress on the first syllable.
solution:
reSIST
SALon
conFIRM
inTACT
subject: English Language

question:
In each of the following sentences, the word that receives the emphatic stress is written in capital letters . From the options lettered A to D, choose the one which the sentence is the appropriate answer.
Lambusa TOOK OFF the wig.
solution: When answering emphatic stress questions, the option that does not have the "stressed word" becomes the correct answer.

options.c: Did Lambusa take off a wig?
options.d: Did Lambusa take off a ring?

subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the underlined word in each sentence.
During the maths test, some students got an approximate score of 20.
solution: The word 'approximate' means something that is close to the actual, but not completely accurate or exact. Therefore, the opposite of 'approximate' is 'precise', which means marked by exactness and accuracy of detail.
subject: English Language

question:
In each of the following sentences, there is one underlined word. From the list of words lettered A to D, choose the one that is most nearly opposite in meaning to the underlined word.
The test shows that the lumps are malignant.
solution:
When something is malignant, it is harmful, malevolent, injurious. The antonym (opposite) of this is benign.
N.B: Malignant is also synonymous to cancerous.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the word that has the same rhyme scheme.

sweet
solution: The correct answer is - suite. It has the same rhyming pattern with the word - suite. Also, they are homophones.
subject: English Language

question:
The primary stress is indicated by writing the syllable on which it occurs in capital letters. From the words lettered A to D choose the one that has the correct stress.
presidency
solution: The correct stress for the word 'presidency' is 'PRE-si-den-cy'. In English, stress means pronouncing one syllable of a multisyllabic word with greater emphasis (stress) than the other syllables in the word. The primary stress in a word is the syllable that is pronounced with the most force. In the word 'presidency', the first syllable 'pre' is pronounced with the most force, hence it has the primary stress.
subject: English Language

question:
This question is based on "The Life Changer" novel.

How old is Bint?
solution: Bint is five years of age
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the underlined word in each sentence.
The abeyance of school activities last semester was a reason for concern.
solution: 'Abeyance' means a state of temporary disuse or suspension. Its antonyms include resume, continue, recommence and reopen.
subject: English Language

question:
From the words lettered A to D below, choose the word or group of words that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word as it is used in the passage.

Mr. Brown is often described as an astute businessman
solution: An astute person is someone who is clever and quick at seeing what to do in a particular situation. Such person can also be described as shrewd.
subject: English Language

question: According to the passage, astronauts
solution: The passage mentions that astronauts may have some slight foreboding on the contingency of their non-return. This implies that they are aware of the risks involved in their journey, including the possibility that they may not return from space.

options.b: always do what is expected of them
options.c: know they may not return from their journey into space

section:
Read the passage carefully and answer this question.

May your road be rough. I am not cursing you: I am wishing you what I wish myself every year, I therefore repeat, may you have a hard time this year. May there be troubles for you this year. If you are not sure of what to say back, why not just say "same to you" – I ask for no more.

Our successes are conditioned by the amount of risk we are about to take. Earlier today, I visited a local farmer about five kilometers from where I live. He could not have been 55, but he said he was already too old to farm vigorously. He still suffered, he said, from the energy he displayed as a farmer in his younger days. Around his hut were two pepperbushes. There were cocoyam growing around him. There were snail shells which had given him meat. There must have been more snails around the banana trees than I saw. He hardly ever went to town to buy things. He was self-sufficient. The car, the television or radio and the newspaper were things he could live without. He had no ambition whatsoever, he told me.

I am not sure if you are already envious of him, but were we all to revert to such a life, we would be driven back like aimless sheep to cave dwelling. On the other hand, try to put yourself in the shoes of the Russian or American astronauts. Any moment you are shot into space, you have to be mentally alert, else, if you forget what to do, one of the things that might happen to you is that you could forever become a satellite going round until you die of starvation, and even then, your dead body would continue the gyration.

Naturally, they may have some slight foreboding on the contingency of their non-return. However, it is their courage for going in spite of these apprehensions that makes the world hail them so loudly today.
(Akinyemi, A., Olupe, F., & Adetutu, S. (2012): Rubrics of English Language for Schools and Colleges. Divine Glory Printers, Abeokuta.)
subject: English Language

question: The farmer in the passage ......
solution: The passage describes the farmer as self-sufficient. He grows his own food, doesn't often go to town to buy things, and can live without modern conveniences like cars, televisions, radios, and newspapers. This suggests that he used to be self-sufficient.

options.b: had a dream to expand his farm
options.c: is more envied than the astronauts

question:
From the options lettered A-D, choose the option that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word.
I have an aversion for war movies.
solution: The word 'aversion' refers to a strong dislike or disinclination. Therefore, the option that is nearest in meaning to 'aversion' is 'dislike'.

question:
Choose the option that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word(s)
When night fell, we had to hasten to the motor park.
solution: Hasten means to move or travel hurriedly. Its synonyms include hurry, hurtle, haste, dash, race and bolt.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the word that has the same rhyme scheme.
laugh
solution: The word 'laugh' rhymes with 'half'. Rhyme scheme refers to the pattern of sounds that repeats at the end of a line or verse.

question: The prisoner finally ........ that he drank to ........ on the night he committed the murder
solution: The correct answer is 'conceded/excess'. The first blank needs a verb that means 'admit something reluctantly', which is 'conceded'. The second blank needs a noun that means 'an amount of something that is more than necessary', which is 'excess'. The other options do not fit the context of the sentence.

solution: The correct answer is 'outstanding'. In this context, the coach is asking for the Bright Rangers to be paid their outstanding salaries. 'Left over' implies that there is some salary that has not been used or claimed, which is not the case here. 'Owed' could potentially work, but it is less formal and less precise than 'outstanding'. 'Late' implies that the salaries are delayed, which is not necessarily the case - they could be unpaid for other reasons.

/θ/

Eating fresh fruits and vegetables is beneficial to one's health while eating processed foods is ......

question:
Select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

The stadium had a capacity crowd in spite of the weather.
solution: The sentence implies that despite the weather conditions, the stadium was filled to capacity. This suggests that the crowd defied the weather to fill the stadium. Therefore, the option 'The crowd defied the weather to fill the stadium' best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

options.a: The stadium was not filled because of the weather
options.b: The crowd defied the weather to fill the stadium
options.c: The crowd loved the weather at the stadium
options.d: The weather favoured the crowd at the stadium

subject: English Language

options.c: The conference is held twice every year
options.d: The conference is held twice in two years

question:
In this question, choose the option that best completes the gap(s).

The proprietor of our school with his administrator ------------ expected yesterday.
solution: The correct answer is - was. "with" in the sentence above only introduces the "attachment" of false subjects. Other words that introduce false subjects are: as well as, in conjunction with, alongside, together with etc. Whenever any of these is used in a sentence, it is the noun or pronoun that comes before any of these false subject introducers that will determine the choice of verb to be used.

From Ummi's narration, what exactly is the "Note of Salvation"?

question:

The acronym "EMEC" as used in the novel means

question: What option best fills the gap labelled 6
solution: In mining, "tunnels" are pathways that connect different parts of a mine.
section:
The passage below has gaps. Immediately after each gap, four options are provided. Choose the most appropriate option for each gap. 

In modern mining, there is a network of __6__ [A. way B. tunnels C. paths D. avenues] and passages, with openings and walls which in the dim light seem very bewildering. Right across the wall at the end of the inky black passage, the __7__ [A. miners B. toilers C. workers D. foreman] approach. The coal __8__ [A. front B. slope C. obstacle D. face] is neatly cut __9__ [A. slit B. gash C. bare D. aperture]. It extends level to a height of rather less than three feet. The rays of the miner's lamp shine on steel roof __10__ [A. beams B. supports C. spurs D. stays]. Thousands of feet of rock and earth are pressing down on those steel plates and __11__ [A. props B. wedges C. poles D. stakes], and the miners have to crawl through the small, deep opening in the wall to get to the coal in the mine that we are considering, the __12__ [A. gash B. hole C. seam D. opening] is nearly four feet thick and is being worked by a combination of __13__ [A. digging B. manpower C. machinery D. spade] and explosives. A mechanical __14__ [A. slit B. gash C. knife D. mesh] saws into the coal to a depth of four or five feet, thus separating it from its base. Explosives pushed into holes drilled at intervals in the coal are __15__ [A. fused B. extinguished C. detonated D. stuck] by remote control and blow the separated section of the coal to pieces.
subject: English Language

section:
The passage below has gaps. Immediately after each gap, four options are provided. Choose the most appropriate option for each gap. 

In modern mining, there is a network of __6__ [A. way B. tunnels C. paths D. avenues] and passages, with openings and walls which in the dim light seem very bewildering. Right across the wall at the end of the inky black passage, the __7__ [A. miners B. toilers C. workers D. foreman] approach. The coal __8__ [A. front B. slope C. obstacle D. face] is neatly cut __9__ [A. slit B. gash C. bare D. aperture]. It extends level to a height of rather less than three feet. The rays of the miner's lamp shine on steel roof __10__ [A. beams B. supports C. spurs D. stays]. Thousands of feet of rock and earth are pressing down on those steel plates and __11__ [A. props B. wedges C. poles D. stakes], and the miners have to crawl through the small, deep opening in the wall to get to the coal in the mine that we are considering, the __12__ [A. gash B. hole C. seam D. opening] is nearly four feet thick and is being worked by a combination of __13__ [A. digging B. manpower C. machinery D. spade] and explosives. A mechanical __14__ [A. slit B. gash C. knife D. mesh] saws into the coal to a depth of four or five feet, thus separating it from its base. Explosives pushed into holes drilled at intervals in the coal are __15__ [A. fused B. extinguished C. detonated D. stuck] by remote control and blow the separated section of the coal to pieces.
subject: English Language

question: What option best fills the gap labelled 9
solution: "Bare" describes the exposed or uncovered coal surface. Other options do not fit the description appropriately.

question: From the passage, it can be inferred that
solution: The passage explains that the type of weathering depends on climatic conditions: physical or mechanical weathering occurs with temperature and water, while chemical weathering thrives in hot climates.

options.a: Climate and weathering are not related
options.b: Climate determines the type of weathering
options.c: Weathering can only occur where the temperature is cool and water is available
options.d: Weathering is possible only in a place where the temperature is hot

section:
Read the passage below and answer each questions that follow:

Weathering, which is the gradual breaking down or disintegration of rocks into smaller particles or fragments by either physical or chemical process, can be affected by many factors. Generally speaking, the climatic condition of a particular area has a great effect on the weathering. Physical or mechanical weathering needs temperature and water. 

Chemical weathering, on the other hand, thrives where the climate is hot. We were all taught all these in the four walls of the classroom. I made a visit to my village to see these scientific discoveries. I noticed big trees with their roots penetrating into rocks and breaking them. I initially wondered and puzzled seeing trees growing from the rock. 

A similar thing happened to the soil sampling surrounding where there were rocks. I could see the rock particles mixing up with the rest of the soil.
subject: English Language

question: A suitable title for the passage above is
solution: The passage focuses on the process of weathering, its types (physical/mechanical and chemical), and the factors that influence it, particularly climatic conditions. Therefore, "Weathering" is the most appropriate title.

options.a: he saw big trees growing from the rocks
options.b: he remembered what he learnt in school
options.c: he was where the atmosphere was cool
options.d: he was left alone wandering in a desert

question: Learning in the four walls of a classroom implies
solution: Learning within the "four walls of a classroom" refers to formal education, which is structured and organised and takes place in a school.

options.a: rock particles cannot be found in the soil
options.b: rock particles can be found in the clay soil

question:
In the following question, select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. 

He was building a castle in the air.
solution: The phrase "building a castle in the air" is an idiom that refers to imagining or planning something fanciful, unrealistic or highly unlikely to happen.

options.b: He was imagining a terrible future
options.c: He was imagining a possible future as a famous architect
options.d: He was imagining a wonderful and rather impossible future

subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. 

Jude appears to sometimes enjoy playing the devil's advocate.
solution: The phrase "playing the devil's advocate" is an idiomatic expression that means arguing for a position one may not necessarily believe in to provoke debate or test the strength of an opposing argument.

options.a: Jude seems to enjoy acting as the devil in films
options.b: Jude seems to enjoy playing the game
options.c: Jude seems to enjoy advancing the unpopular side of an argument
options.d: Jude seems to always enjoy appearing differently

subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

Yerima always holds his own in any situation he finds himself.
solution: The phrase "holds his own in any situation" means that Yerima is capable of maintaining his position, handling himself well, or standing firm regardless of the circumstances. This means that he has confidence and composure.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

Titi was the cynosure of all eyes at the party.
solution: The phrase "cynosure of all eyes" means someone who is the focus of attention or admiration. In this context, it suggests that Titi drew everyone's attention at the party.

options.b: She was the centre of attraction
options.d: She was eager to leave the party

subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

Let this case stand over for the present, the lawyer pleaded.
solution: The phrase "stand over for the present" means to temporarily delay or postpone something to a later time. It is used in legal or formal contexts to request that a matter be deferred.

options.a: The case should be discussed immediately
options.b: The case should be struck out
options.d: The case is pending in court

subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

The judge declared the two accused guilty.
solution: The phrase "declared the two accused guilty" means that the judge found the two accused individuals responsible for the crime. The opposite of guilty is innocent.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

A prosperous lawyer is definitely hardworking.
solution: The word "prosperous" means successful or thriving in terms of wealth or financial stability. The opposite of prosperous is poor.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

Fake news is rife on social media platforms.
solution: In this context, "fake news" refers to misleading or false information. The opposite of fake news would be authentic news, meaning real or genuine.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

The children's room was messy when we entered.
solution: The word "messy" means disorganized or untidy. The opposite of messy is tidy, which means clean and organized.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

The judge pronounced the suspect guilty.
solution: The word "guilty" means found responsible for a crime. The opposite of guilty is acquitted, which means to be declared not guilty of a crime.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

The lecturer's wife has become quite unconscionable.
solution: The word "unconscionable" refers to behaviour that is immoral or unethical and beyond what is reasonable or just. The nearest in meaning to the word unconscionable is unbecoming, which means inappropriate or not in keeping with the standards of behaviour expected in a particular context, though less severe than unconscionable.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

He bequeathed his company to his last daughter.
solution: The word "bequeathed" means to leave something (especially property or possessions) to someone in a will. The nearest meaning to bequeathed is left, as in leaving something behind for someone.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

She went to the court to seek redress for the wrong done to her.
solution: The word "redress" means seeking justice or compensation for a wrong or injury. In this context, redress means asking for compensation to make up for the harm or injustice done.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

The attempt by the robbers to break into the bank was futile.
solution: The word "futile" means something that is pointless or without success. The nearest meaning to futile is fruitless, which also means producing no results or being unsuccessful.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

The newly employed teachers gave their work rapt attention.
solution: Paying "rapt" attention means paying full attention or being fully absorbed in something. The nearest meaning to rapt attention is concentrated, which refers to focusing all one's attention on a task or activity.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

Sally assisted me in buying my _____ yesterday.
solution: The phrase "very first party dress" is the grammatically correct option because the word "very" is used to emphasise the first party dress, which is the key element in the sentence.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

You know that you ____ it before tomorrow.
solution: The phrase "ought to do" means that there is an expectation or obligation to complete something by tomorrow.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

Maryam, the girl in my neighbourhood, treats people as if she _____ better than they are.
solution: The sentence uses a hypothetical situation (Maryam treating people as if she is better than they are), and in such cases, we use the subjunctive mood, which requires the verb "were" instead of "is" or "was" when expressing unreal or hypothetical conditions.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

But for the timely intervention of the police, the thief ______ by the angry mob.
solution: The phrase "But for the timely intervention of the police" indicates a hypothetical situation in the past, which suggests that the thief would have been lynched if the police hadn't intervened. In such a situation, the correct form to use is the past perfect conditional, "would have been lynched."
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

My father loves bringing ___ old memories.
solution: The phrase "bringing up old memories" is an idiomatic expression that means to mention or recall past memories.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

Women always ______ themselves in beautiful apparel.
solution: The word "adorn" means to decorate or add beauty to oneself or something.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

The movie wasn't fantastic, was it? _____
solution: The appropriate answer to this is "No, it wasn't" to show agreement with the speaker. Question tags are used to elicit agreement from a listener and therefore the most appropriate response should be one of agreement.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

My uncle is not sure ____ the strange guests were.
solution: The sentence refers to people, specifically the "strange guests," so the correct word to use is "who", which is used to refer to people.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

Everyone in class wanted the Literature teacher to ______ the point of view of the _______.
solution: "Expatiate" means to elaborate or explain something in detail. "Playwright" is someone who writes plays. Other options have wrong spellings of the words.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

____ of his lateness, Okupe gained from the coaching.
solution: "In spite of" is used to show contrast with something (in this case, Okupe's lateness). The phrase means that despite being late, Okupe still benefited from the coaching.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

The man is not as powerful as he once was, his influence has considerably _______
solution: "Diminished" means reduced in size, strength or importance. It shows that the man's influence has lessened over time.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

I can't lend him my books. I ______ know him.
solution: "Scarcely" means "hardly" or "barely," which means that the speaker knows the person only to a very limited extent, making it unlikely for the speaker to lend the books.
subject: English Language

question: Honeybees have two characteristics similar to humans.
solution: The passage mentions that honeybees are social insects and live together in hives. Additionally, it highlights that they communicate with each other through a unique dancing movement to share information about food sources. This reflects a social structure and communication, which is a characteristic similar to humans.

options.a: they have both male and female sexes
options.b: they have a caste system and work for a living
options.d: they are social and communicate with one another

section:
Read the passage carefully and answer the questions that follow.

The honeybee includes a group of insects that in a broad sense comprises all bees that make honey. They are social insects and live together in hives. The honeybee is remarkable for the dancing movement it performs in the hive to communicate information to its fellow bees about the location, distance, size, and quality of a particular food source in the surrounding area. There are two honeybee sexes, male and female, and two female castes. The two female castes are known as workers and queens. The males, or drones, are larger than the workers and are present only for about eight weeks, in early summer. The workers and queen have stingers, whereas the drones are stingless. The queens' unfertilised eggs develop into drones, whereas fertilised eggs develop into females, which may be either workers or virgin queens. Eggs destined to become queens are deposited in queen cells. After hatching, the virgin queens are fed royal jelly. When not fed a diet consisting solely of royal jelly, virgin queens will develop into workers. 

Adapted from Britannica, T. Editors of Encyclopedia (2020, March 24), honeybee. Encyclopedia Britannica. 

https://www.britannica.com/animal/honeybee
subject: English Language

question: According to the passage, it is not all honeybees that produce
solution:
According to the passage, it is not all honeybees that produce eggs. The passage states that only the queens lay eggs:

"The queens' unfertilised eggs develop into drones, whereas fertilised eggs develop into females, which may be either workers or virgin queens."

subject: English Language

question: Another word for the word destined according to the passage would be
solution: In the passage, the word "destined" refers to the predetermined fate or purpose of the eggs in the honeybee colony. It mentions that "eggs destined to become queens" are deposited in queen cells, meaning these eggs are predetermined to develop into queens.
section:
Read the passage carefully and answer the questions that follow.

The honeybee includes a group of insects that in a broad sense comprises all bees that make honey. They are social insects and live together in hives. The honeybee is remarkable for the dancing movement it performs in the hive to communicate information to its fellow bees about the location, distance, size, and quality of a particular food source in the surrounding area. There are two honeybee sexes, male and female, and two female castes. The two female castes are known as workers and queens. The males, or drones, are larger than the workers and are present only for about eight weeks, in early summer. The workers and queen have stingers, whereas the drones are stingless. The queens' unfertilised eggs develop into drones, whereas fertilised eggs develop into females, which may be either workers or virgin queens. Eggs destined to become queens are deposited in queen cells. After hatching, the virgin queens are fed royal jelly. When not fed a diet consisting solely of royal jelly, virgin queens will develop into workers. 

Adapted from Britannica, T. Editors of Encyclopedia (2020, March 24), honeybee. Encyclopedia Britannica.

question:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

The paper stated that the driver of the lorry was helping the police in their investigation.
solution:
"The paper stated that the driver of the lorry was helping the police in their investigation" means that the driver is cooperating with the police and providing information to assist their investigation.

When a news report says someone is "helping police with their inquiries" or "helping in their investigation," it is a polite way of saying that the person is being questioned by the police in connection with an incident or crime. It doesn't necessarily mean the person is under arrest, but they are providing information that the police believe is relevant to their investigation.

options.a: joining the police in their investigation
options.b: being questioned at the police station
options.c: helping the police to drive around
options.d: helping the police who were injured

subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

It is not in my place to keep late, no matter what happens.
solution: It means that the speaker avoids being late under any circumstances.

options.a: I am not used to keeping late
options.b: I am a habitual latecomer to any event
options.c: I do not get to anywhere later than expected
options.d: I do not really care whether I get to an event late or not

subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. 

Tayo had to separate himself neck and crop from his old friend.
solution: The phrase "separate himself neck and crop" means to sever ties entirely or completely break away.

options.a: He became scared of his old friend
options.b: He became suspicious of his old friend
options.c: He completely severed from his old friend
options.d: He completely forgave his old friend's offences

subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. 

One of the prerequisites for success in examination is burning the midnight oil.
solution: The phrase "burning the midnight oil" is an idiom that means working or studying late into the night.

options.a: needs to study late into the night
options.c: one should burn all the candles
options.d: one needs to study at will

subject: English Language

question:
In each of the following questions, choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

The instructions given to him are vague.
solution: The word vague means unclear or not specific. The opposite of this is clear, which means easy to understand or unambiguous.
subject: English Language

question:
In each of the following questions, choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

Due to old age, mother has become taciturn.
solution: The word taciturn means someone who is habitually quiet or reserved in speech. The opposite is garrulous, which describes someone who is excessively talkative.
subject: English Language

question:
In each of the following questions, choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

She remembered how reticent her father had been about his past.
solution: Reticent means reserved or unwilling to share thoughts or feelings. The opposite, forthcoming, describes someone who is open, communicative, and willing to share information.
subject: English Language

question:
In each of the following questions, choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

The trial of the former governor was stopped and the earlier judgement was declared null and void.
solution: Null and void means invalid or without legal force. The opposite, credible, suggests validity or trustworthiness.
subject: English Language

question:
In each of the following questions, choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

Unemployment among school leavers has reached epidemic proportions.
solution: Epidemic proportions refer to something that has grown to an extensive or widespread level, similar to how an epidemic affects a large population.
subject: English Language

question:
In each of the following questions, choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

At a crucial time, one should be cautious in taking decisive action.
solution: The word "decisive" means having the power to make firm decisions or determine an outcome. In the sentence, "taking decisive action" refers to making an important and firm decision at a crucial time. Significant is nearest in meaning because a decisive action is one that has an important impact.
subject: English Language

question:
In each of the following questions, choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

The assistant class teacher was told to stand in for Mrs Chukwudi.
solution: "Stand in for" means to take someone's place or support them temporarily, which is closest in meaning to "support."
subject: English Language

question:
In each of the following questions, choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

The board Chairman cherishes punctuality at the meeting.
solution: "Cherishes" means to value or admire something, so "admires" is the closest in meaning.
subject: English Language

question:
In each of the following questions, select the option that best completes the gap. 

The touts ____ arrested during the riot.
solution: The subject "touts" is plural, so the correct form of the verb is "were."
subject: English Language

question:
In each of the following questions, select the option that best completes the gap. 

The ripe orange was beyond his ______.
solution: The phrase "beyond his reach" means that something is too far away or inaccessible to him.
subject: English Language

question:
In each of the following questions, select the option that best completes the gap. 

The students _____ the planned meeting with the new principal.
solution: "Boycotted" means to intentionally avoid or refuse to participate in something, typically as an expression of protest. This fits the context of students not participating in a planned meeting with the new principal.
subject: English Language

question:
In each of the following questions, select the option that best completes the gap. 

Take your share and give them _____
solution: "Theirs" is the possessive form of "they," indicating something that belongs to them. In this case, it refers to the share belonging to others.
subject: English Language

The children chased all the _______ away.

question:

He went ____ the corridor, looking for the principal's office.
solution:
The sentence should read: "He went along the corridor, looking for the principal's office."

"Along" is used to describe movement in a line or path, such as along a corridor.

This phrase means that a celebration is needed or appropriate in this situation.

bath - /bɑːθ/

care - /keə/
subject: English Language

question:
In each of the following questions, choose the most appropriate stress pattern from the options. The stressed syllable is written in capital letters. 

immunity
solution: For multi-syllabic words that end in '-ty', the stress tends to fall on the ante-penultimate syllable (i.e. the third syllable from the last). Therefore, the stress placement would be iMMUnity.
subject: English Language

question:
In each of the following questions, choose the most appropriate stress pattern from the options. The stressed syllable is written in capital letters. 

radioactive
solution: Words that end with -ive have their stress on the penultimate syllable (i.e. on the second to the last syllable). Therefore, the stress placement is radioACtive.
subject: English Language

question:
In each of the following questions, the word in capital letter has the emphatic stress. Choose the option to which the given words relate. 

His reign was noted for PEACE.
solution: Emphatic stress is the placement of greater stress on a particulate word in a sentence for clarification or emphasis. The question requires you to select the question that incites the response given. The word "Peace" is emphasised in the response, and so you are to look for an option that has a word that is different from "peace". The answer is option A because the emphatic stress on PEACE contrasts with wealth in the question, thereby validating and emphasizing the word PEACE in the response.

options.a: Was his reign noted for wealth?
options.b: Whose house is noted for peace?
options.c: Was his reign examined for peace?
options.d: Whose reign is noted for peace?

subject: English Language

question:
In each of the following questions, the word in capital letter has the emphatic stress. Choose the option to which the given words relate. 

Bala ate a slice of BREAD.
solution: Emphatic stress is the placement of greater stress on a particulate word in a sentence for clarification or emphasis. The question requires you to select the question that incites the response given. The word "bread" is emphasised in the response, and so you are to look for an option that has a word that is different from "bread". The answer is option D because the emphatic stress on BREAD contrasts with cake in the question, thereby validating and emphasizing the word BREAD in the response.

options.a: Did Bala drink a slice of bread?
options.b: Did Bala eat a loaf of bread?
options.c: Did Olu eat a slice of bread?
options.d: Did Bala eat a slice of cake?

subject: English Language

question: The word anguish as used in the passage means
solution: In the context of the passage, anguish refers to extreme suffering or pain, both physical and emotional, as described by the harrowing experiences of the people.
section: On that fateful day, they had pursued everyone in the neighbouring villages. Later, they came to ours and pursued us to the mountains. Everyone ran for dear life. We knew what we were up against. They blocked the mountains for two months. We were stuck there, praying and fasting, asking our God to show us a way out. God heard our prayers. One day, we left around 12 midnight from the mountains. Without shoes and clothes, we just left. As we left the mountains that day and descended, we almost came in contact with the insurgents. We hid in the bush till they left around 2 o'clock. We started to walk to Cameroon. We travelled for two days across several labyrinths. We were in anguish. Our children's feet were swollen. Some of them bitten by snakes died because we were not able to carry them to hospital for treatment.
subject: English Language

question: The phrase on that fateful day is
solution: The phrase on that fateful day modifies the verb had pursued by providing information about when the action occurred, making it an adverbial phrase.
section: On that fateful day, they had pursued everyone in the neighbouring villages. Later, they came to ours and pursued us to the mountains. Everyone ran for dear life. Some of them bitten by snakes died because we were not able to carry them to hospital for treatment.
subject: English Language

question: From the passage, it is clear that
solution: This is evident from the passage where it is stated, "Some of them bitten by snakes died because we were not able to carry them to hospital for treatment."

options.a: everyone from the writer's village arrived Cameroon alive
options.b: everyone from the writer's village arrived the mountains alive
options.c: some children were not able to make it to Cameroon
options.d: all children made it to Cameroon alive despite the snakebites

question: What does the writer mean by we knew what we were up against?
solution: The phrase shows the writer's awareness of the dangerous and violent nature of the insurgents pursuing them.

options.a: The height of the mountains they were running to
options.c: The difficulty of life on the mountains
options.d: The distance from the mountains to Cameroon

solution: The phrase "travelling across several labyrinths" suggests a challenging and confusing journey. It shows the difficulty in finding their way to Cameroon.

options.a: it was easy for them to locate the paths leading to Cameroon
options.b: Cameroon is a straight distance from the mountains
options.c: they arrived Cameroon after two days of crossing several rivers
options.d: they had a complicated and tortuous journey through the bush

question:
Select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. 

It is highly improbable that it will snow in June.
solution: The word "improbable" means "unlikely to happen". The other options either exaggerate the sentence or does not convey the information in the sentence at all.

options.a: It is highly unlikely that it will snow in June
options.b: It is possible that it will snow in June
options.c: It will never snow in June
options.d: The snowfall will probably be high in June

subject: English Language

question:
Select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. 

He played his wife false by inviting his mistress to the house as his cousin.
solution: The phrase "played his wife false" means he acted deceitfully toward her, and the fact that he invited his mistress to the house as his cousin implies he was unfaithful to his wife.

options.a: he brought his cousin to his wife's room
options.b: His cousin and his wife were friends
options.c: He cheated on his wife with his mistress
options.d: He helped his wife with his mistress

subject: English Language

question:
Select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. 

Driving in Lagos is a difficult task.
solution: The phrase "a difficult task" implies that driving in Lagos is extremely challenging. A "herculean task"  is a task that requires great strength and effort. It is synonymous with a difficult task.

options.a: It is an easy task to drive in Lagos
options.b: It is a difficult task driving Lagos
options.c: It is a herculean task driving in Lagos
options.d: It is not a difficult task to drive in Lagos

subject: English Language

question:
Select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. 

I am given a free hand to run this business.
solution: The phrase "given a free hand" means being granted the autonomy or freedom to make decisions and manage something without interference. Hence, option A correctly explains the conveyed meaning.

options.a: I am allowed to do my own business
options.d: I alone can choose my executives

subject: English Language

question:
Select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. 

The government has promised to pay our gratuity, we need to have our fingers crossed.
solution: The phrase "have our fingers crossed" means to remain hopeful or wish for a positive outcome. Therefore, option C best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

options.b: We should be united in purpose

subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

She looks very pale.
solution: "Ruddy" refers to a healthy, red or flushed complexion, which is the opposite of "pale" (a lighter or less healthy complexion). Therefore, option A is the opposite in meaning to "pale."
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

Before going to bed, make sure you extinguish all the lights.
solution: "Extinguish" means to turn off or put out. The opposite of turning off the lights (extinguishing) would be to "switch on" the lights. Therefore, option C is the opposite in meaning to "extinguish."
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

Ebere's indolence contrasts with her sister's.
solution: "Indolence" refers to a state of laziness or avoidance of work. The opposite of laziness or indolence is "diligence," which means careful and persistent work or effort. Therefore, option C is the opposite in meaning to "indolence."
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

The roads in major cities are broad.
solution: The word "broad" refers to something wide or spacious. The opposite of broad, in this context, is "narrow," which refers to something thin or restricted in width. Therefore, option D is the opposite in meaning to "broad."
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

He gave an indifferent look at his dismissal.
solution: "Indifferent" means showing a lack of interest or concern. The opposite of indifference, in this case, would be "concerned," meaning showing care or attention. Therefore, option B is the correct opposite in meaning.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that is nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

The official has declared the election inconclusive.
solution: "Inconclusive" means something that does not provide clear results or answers. "Indeterminate" carries a similar meaning, indicating something that is uncertain or undefined, making it the closest in meaning.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

His reckless remark did not go unnoticed.
solution: A "reckless" remark refers to a statement made without careful thought or consideration and that is spontaneous or careless. "Unguarded" means something that is made without restraint or caution.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

The old fire station has obviously become derelict.
solution: "Derelict" means something that is in a state of disrepair or abandonment, which closely relates to "moribund," meaning something that is in a dying or decaying state. Both terms suggest neglect or decline.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

Death is inevitable.
solution: "Inevitable" means something that cannot be avoided or prevented, which is synonymous with "unavoidable." Both terms suggest that an event or outcome is certain to happen.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

I had to put up my house as _____ for the bank loan.
solution: When someone offers something of value, like a house, in exchange for a loan, it is referred to as "collateral." This is a common practice where the collateral serves as security for the loan in case the borrower fails to repay it.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

The beggar would be grateful for even a _____ of bread.
solution: The phrase "even a crust of bread" is a common expression used to suggest that someone would be grateful for even a small, humble portion of bread, such as the crust, which is often considered the least desirable part.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

These properties were ____ sale before the fire gutted them.
solution: The phrase "up for sale" is commonly used to express that something is available or being offered for sale. In this case, it means the properties were available for sale before the fire destroyed them.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

The ______ of armed robbery has been on the increase.
solution: "Incidence" refers to the occurrence or frequency of an event, typically used in the context of events like crimes or diseases. Incidence on the other hand refers to a specific event or occurrence often unexpected or of a particular nature. In this sentence, we're discussing the general frequency or occurrence of armed robbery. This makes incidence the correct option in this context.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

She hurried off ______ and came back.
solution: "Fast" is the correct adverb form to describe the manner in which she hurried. "Fast" itself is both an adjective and an adverb. "Fastly" is not the standard adverb form.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

Justina used to be the best-behaved girl, ____?
solution: In questions like this, the auxiliary verb used in the tag question should match the auxiliary verb or the verb used in the main sentence. All sentences have an auxiliary verb even though it is not visible. Sentences without visible auxiliary verbs carry the auxiliary verb "did" as in "She did used to be the best-behaved girl". Therefore, the correct tag question is "didn't she."
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

It was the _______ of brakes that drew our attention.
solution: The phrase "screeching of brakes" is a common expression used to describe the high-pitched, loud noise made by brakes when a vehicle is stopped suddenly.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

Everyone knew the woman's _______ before his phone went off.
solution: The word "itinerary" refers to a planned route or journey that details the travel schedule or events. The other options are misspellings of the word.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

Put Chioma's meal in the _______ pack.
solution:
A. shiny, new, red lunch

The other should be:

  1. Opinion or observation (shiny)
  2. Age or condition (new)
  3. Colour (red)
    Thus, "shiny, new, red" follows the correct order of adjectives before the noun "lunch."
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

I was glad he called my attention ____ the matter.
solution: The correct preposition after the verb "call attention" is "to." We say "call someone's attention to something," meaning to make them aware of or focus on something.
subject: English Language

hurt

question:
Choose the appropriate stress pattern from the options. The stressed syllables are written in capital letters. 

embodiment
solution: /ɪmˈbɒd.i.mənt/
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the appropriate stress pattern from the options. The stressed syllables are written in capital letters. 

declamation
solution: /ˌdek.ləˈmeɪ.ʃən/
subject: English Language

question:
The word in capital letters has the emphatic stress. Choose the options to which the given sentence relates. 

The architect did the DESIGN.
solution: Emphatic stress is the placement of greater stress on a particular word in a sentence or utterance for emphasis, correction or clarification. To test for emphatic stress, the contrasting question should affirm or negate the specific word in capital letters ("DESIGN"). Option D contrasts "design" with "draft," making it the appropriate response to confirm emphasis.

options.b: Was the design done by the engineer?
options.c: Did the architect destroy the design?
options.d: Did the architect do the draft?

subject: English Language

question:
The word in capital letters has the emphatic stress. Choose the options to which the given sentence relates. 

My only SON speaks like an orator.
solution: For emphatic stress on "SON," the contrasting question should challenge "son" by substituting it with a different noun (e.g., "daughter"). Option B correctly contrasts "son" with "daughter," confirming the emphasis.

options.a: Does my only son speak like an illiterate?
options.b: Does my only daughter speak like an orator?
options.c: Does my only son read like an orator?
options.d: Does your only son speak like an orator?

subject: English Language

question: According to the passage, students perform poorly in examinations because
solution: The passage explicitly mentions that one of the main causes of mass failure in examinations is students' inability to imbibe a good reading culture. This is highlighted as the primary reason learners struggle with comprehension and examination performance.

options.b: they fail to identify the ethics of reading
options.c: they are too pre-occupied with social activities
options.d: of their inability to imbibe good reading culture

section:
Read the passage carefully and answer the question that follows

One of the basic language skills is reading. In any literate society, reading is a very essential aspect of effective communication in daily activities. Reading has been many things to many people. To some people, it is like looking at printed words while most students see it as a tedious task that has to be performed in order to pass a particular examination. Reading, like any other term, has been described in different ways, such as "Reading as an intellectual activity directed towards the extraction of information from a written piece". One of the causes of mass failure in various examinations among candidates is their inability to imbibe a good reading culture. With poor reading habits, learners cannot comprehend the emphasised hence adequate textbooks should be provided for students.

(Adapted from Ayo Banjo et al (2020) New Oxford Secondary English Course for Senior Secondary School. Ibadan: Oxford University)
subject: English Language

question: It is implied in the passage that
solution: The passage emphasises the importance of a good reading culture and its connection to effective comprehension. This implies that students who develop the habit of reading are more likely to grasp the writer's intentions and understand the text better.

options.a: students who cultivate reading habit easily comprehend the intention of the writer
options.b: the understanding of the writer will be strenuous for the students who prepare well enough
options.c: students who fail to imbibe a good reading culture will find it easy to know the intention of the writer
options.d: a student can easily understand the writer's intention whether he imbibes a good reading culture or not

question: From the passage, it is implied that
solution: The passage states, "most students see it as a tedious task that has to be performed in order to pass a particular examination." This implies that the majority of students perceive reading as a challenging activity.

options.a: few students see reading as a difficult task
options.b: all students see reading as a simple task
options.c: some students see reading as a difficult task
options.d: most students see reading as a difficult task

question: Fill the gap labelled 6
solution: The correct phrase is "impart knowledge," meaning to share or communicate knowledge to others.
section:
The passage below has gap(s), following each gap is four options. For each gap, choose the most appropriate option. 

Teaching is a noble profession. Only those who have the calling to __6__ [A. impact B. impart C. infer D. confer] knowledge is called great teachers. 

The teachers' duty goes beyond the four walls of a classroom. They teach students various topics and __7__ [A. formula B. courses C. classes D. subjects]. The teacher listens to their students express their worries and fears, yet the teacher has to pacify and __8__ [A. care B. guide C. beat D. guard] them through the right paths. 

As a __9__ [A. stakeholder B. shareholder C. officeholder D. cardholder] in the field of education, the teacher ensures that teaching-learning __10__ [A. habit B. function C. process D. policy] takes place in and out of the classroom. The teacher lights the candle for the others to see. He/ she is fast enough to move at the speed of the __11__ [A. guiness B. genius C. heinous D. spacious] and patient enough for the slow-learner to catch up. 

In his care are the __12__ [A. book B. diary C. register D. attendance], the mark book and every other document which must be duly filled. While at home, the teacher still plans his lesson and prepares his teaching __13__ [A. books B. steps C. maps D. aids], all for the purpose of effective teaching-learning process. 

Teachers should not be brushed aside in the society as teaching is the profession that __14__ [A. birthed B. delivered C. approached D. positioned] other professions. Teachers should be given a great __15__ [A. attention B. honour C. privilege D. care] in the society.
subject: English Language

question: FIll the gap labelled 7
solution: Teachers teach various "subjects," which are the areas of study, like mathematics or literature.

solution: A teacher is a "stakeholder" in the education system. This means that they have a vested interest in its success.

question:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentences.

Adeola lives a stone's throw away from the church.
solution: The idiom "a stone's throw" means a very short distance. In this context, it suggests that Adeola's house is very close to the church.

options.a: He lives where stones are thrown to the church
options.b: He lives a long distance from the church
options.c: He lives not too close to the church
options.d: He lives very close to the church

subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentences.

Here he comes, speak of the devil.
solution: The phrase "speak of the devil" is an idiom used when someone being talked about unexpectedly shows up. It does not imply anything about the person being the devil or associated with evil.

options.a: He is scared of seeing the devil
options.b: He is the representation of the devil
options.d: He appears as he is being discussed

subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentences.

Moses won the contest hands down.
solution: The expression "to win hands down" means to achieve victory effortlessly or with little difficulty. It implies that the win was straightforward and without struggle.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentences.

I decided to back up my friend's argument at the meeting.
solution: To "back up" someone means to support or endorse their position, statement or argument. In this context, the speaker is expressing agreement with their friend's argument during the meeting.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

All men in public life should be altruistic.
solution: The word "altruistic" means being selfless or showing a concern for the well-being of others. The opposite of this would be "selfish," which means prioritising one's own needs and interests over others.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

Everyone thought the politician was invincible.
solution: The word "invincible" means unable to be defeated or overcome. The opposite is "surmountable," which means capable of being overcome or defeated.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

Jemila's austere reaction kept everyone on their toes.
solution: The word "austere" refers to something strict, severe, or harsh in manner or attitude. The opposite, "benign," means gentle, kind, or mild in character or approach.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

My grandpa's stories are usually phony in nature.
solution: The word "phony" means fake or not genuine. The opposite of "phony" would be something genuine or based on fact, which is factual.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

Ayo does regular exercise to stretch his mind.
solution: To "stretch" one's mind means to expand or broaden one's mental capacity. The opposite of this would be to "restrict," which means to limit or constrain.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that is nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

She is anxious about her son who has been ill.
solution: "Anxious" means being worried or unsettled, which is closest in meaning to "perturbed," meaning disturbed or troubled.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

Everyone thinks Ojo's decision to be a demagogue is ill-advised.
solution: A demagogue is someone who seeks to gain power by manipulating people's emotions, prejudices or fears. Therefore, the word manipulator is the closest in meaning, as it also implies someone who controls or influences others in a negative way.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that is nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

Most celebrities usually have a tinge of dishonour in their life.
solution: The word tinge refers to a slight trace or hint of something, often used to describe a subtle amount of colour or quality. Tint is a synonym, meaning a slight shade or trace of a particular quality, which fits the context of the sentence.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that is nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

What a grotesque attire.
solution: The word grotesque means something that is unnatural, ugly or disturbing in appearance. Horrid is the nearest in meaning, as it conveys a sense of something unpleasant or repulsive.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that is nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

A vulture was hovering over the dead goat.
solution: The word hovering refers to staying in one place in the air and flying around without moving much.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

My uncle is not sure ____ the strange guests were.
solution: In this sentence, the word "who" is the correct choice because it is used to refer to people. The sentence is asking about the identity of the strange guests, which are people, so "who" is appropriate.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

If her brother ____ here, he would call the police.
solution:
The sentence is a hypothetical or unreal condition, indicating that the situation is not true at the moment. In English, for unreal or hypothetical situations in the present or future, we use the past simple tense (e.g., "were") instead of "was" for all subjects, even with singular subjects like "he" or "she". Therefore, "were" is the correct choice here.

This is an example of the second conditional, which is used to talk about hypothetical situations.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

When they started the fighting, the man who tried to ____ was knocked down.
solution: The verb "intervene" means to get involved in a situation to help or prevent something. In this context, the man who tried to intervene (or stop the fighting) was knocked down.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

Why do children _____ adults' conversations?
solution: The phrase "break into" means to interrupt or enter something, such as a conversation, without permission. It fits the context of the sentence, where children are interrupting adults' conversations.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the gap in the sentence.

Mr Ezeh _______ with some solutions to the issue.
solution: The phrase "came up" means to think of or devise something.
subject: English Language

question:
Select the option that best completes the gap in the sentence. 

Tests reveal that the winning athlete had taken some performance-enhancing ______.
solution: The phrase "performance-enhancing drugs" is a common expression used to refer to substances that athletes use to improve their performance. "Drugs" is the general and most appropriate term in this context.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the gap in the sentence. 

How many eggs were _____ by the hen?
solution: The verb "laid" is the correct past tense form of "lay" (when referring to something like eggs being produced). "Lain" is the past participle of "lie" (as in "to recline"), not "lay."
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the gap in the sentence. 

Are those girls friends of _____?
solution: In this sentence, "yours" is a possessive pronoun, which is used to indicate ownership or relationship.
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

The jury gave ____ verdict.
solution: "Its" is the correct possessive form of the pronoun "it," used to show ownership or association. In this case, the verdict belongs to the jury, so "its" is appropriate. "His" and "her" are possessive pronouns used for male and female subjects, respectively, but the jury is a group and takes the neutral "its."
subject: English Language

question:
Choose the option that has the same vowel sound as the one represented by the letter(s) underlined. 

Mother
solution:
Mother - /ˈmʌð.ə/

Verb - /vɜːb/

Mercy - /ˈmɜː.si/

About - /əˈbaʊt/

question:
Choose the option that has the same consonant sound as the one represented by the letter(s) underlined.

Yesterday
solution:
Yesterday - /ˈjes.tə.deɪ/

Jam - /dʒæm/

Union - /ˈjuː.njən/

Jump - /dʒʌmp/
